Passive Ability.Name: Pickup
Passive Ability.Description:
Attacking Pokémon from the opposing team copies their battle item for 30s. Defeating a wild Pokémon instead copies a random battle item for 30s. The copied item temporarily functions as the user’s battle item, without replacing or affecting the user’s original item. Once an item is copied, this ability will go on cooldown for 30s.

Moving towards Aeos energy or gold coins increases the user's movement speed by 20%. Picking up gold coins increases the user's movement speed for 3s by 40% decaying by 5% every 0.5s to a minimum of 20%, and speeds up the rate at which Pickup's cooldown goes down by 13.5% for each gold coin picked up. Gold coins will stay on the field for 7s. The user can pick up an opposing Meowth's gold coins as if they were their own.

Meowth.Attack: Scratches at opposing Pokémon becoming a boosted attack with every third attack dealing increased damage and dropping a gold coin.

Move 1.Name: Scratch
Move 1.Description: The user scratches a designated Pokémon with its sharp claws, dealing damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Pay Day
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user hurls a gold coin at the designated Pokémon, dealing damage, slowing down the target by 30% for 1.5s, and dropping a gold coin.

2 uses can be kept in reserve, picking up a gold coin reduces the cooldown of a use by 4s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage dealt by this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Fury Swipes
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user applies a coin mark on opposing Pokémon they hit, the coin mark has up to 5 stacks and lasts for 5s. The user swipes twice with its sharp claws at the designated Pokémon, dealing damage, and granting the user a shield for 5s, and an additional shield per hit (up to 5x) for 5s. The number of swipes can be increased up to 5 if the coin mark is at 3 stacks or higher. Subsequent swipes deal half of the damage of the first hit. This attack will stun opposing Pokémon that have 1/2/3+ coin mark stacks for 0.3/0.5/0.8s respectively.

If the maximum number of swipes hit, the cooldowns for Feint, Feint Attack, and Assurance are reduced by 7.5% of the max cooldown per hit, the 5th hit will fully reset the cooldown. If the coin mark is filled up, this move will consume and reset the mark.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage dealt by this move.

Move 2.Name: Feint
Move 2.Description: The user dashes at the designated Pokémon, dealing damage to opponents hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Feint Attack
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user pounces at the designated Pokémon and scratches them with their claws, dealing damage and stunning the target for 0.6s. When this move hits the user is granted a shield for 3s and can quickly move in a chosen direction.

The user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack. If the coin mark is filled up, this move will consume and reset the mark (although this move does not benefit from the coin mark in any fashion).
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Improves the strength of the shield.

Upgrade 2.Name: Assurance
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user applies a coin mark on opposing Pokémon they hit, the coin mark has up to 5 stacks and lasts for 5s. The user charges in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit and restoring the user's HP, furthermore the move will restore additional HP if the targets have a coin mark. The more stacks the coin mark has the more potent the heal.

When this move is used on a Pokémon with a coin mark that has 5 stacks, the damage is doubled. If the coin mark is filled up, this move will consume and reset the mark.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this moves cooldown by 1s.

Unite Move.Name: Gold Coin Barrage
Unite Move.Description:
The user becomes unstoppable and unleashes a barrage of gold coins toward the designated Pokémon from the opposing team, dealing damage in a small area up to 10 times. If another Pokémon on the opposing team stands in the way, they will be hit instead. During the attack, up to 4 coins will periodically drop near Pokémon hit by this move. This move can be cancelled early.

Meowth can move while using this move, but is slowed by 110%.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 80% Movement Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Steadfast
Passive Ability.Description:
Tenacity is increased by 50% while the user has a shield. When the user hits an opposing Pokémon with a basic attack or move, the user's critical-hit rate increases by 5% up to 5 times for a maximum of 25%. This enhanced critical-hit rate remains for up to 8s after the user last dealt damage with a basic attack or move.

As a Farfetch'd, the user will gain 10 Exp. Points when landing critical hits. This Exp. can trigger evolution. Critical hits deal 160% damage rather than 200%.

Sirfetch'd.Attack:
The user attacks by slashing with a leek.

This Pokémon has no boosted attack.

Move 1.Name: Dual Wingbeat
Move 1.Description: The user slashes in the designated direction, if the first hit lands then the user can use this move again within 5s to slam the leek in the designated spot.

Upgrade 1.Name: Fury Cutter
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user unleashes a flurry of jabs in the designated direction, with the damage increasing by 3% each time the move deals damage up to 2 times (6% max). The final hit stuns opposing Pokémon for 0.75s.

Sirfetch'd can move while unleashing the flurry of jabs. If the move is interrupted, 50% of the remaining cooldown is refunded.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Leaf Blade
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user jabs in the designated direction, if the first hit lands then the user can use this move again within 10s to dash and slash in a designated direction, throwing opposing Pokémon hit for 0.5s.

If the first hit of this move misses, the cooldown is instead 2s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Move 2.Name: Quick Attack
Move 2.Description: The user dashes towards the designated Pokémon, dealing damage and gaining a shield for 3s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Brutal Swing
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user throws the leek-leaf shield in an arc, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit. When the leek-leaf shield returns the user gains a shield for 4s. While the leek-leaf shield is mid-flight it can continuously inflict damage to the same opponent every 0.5s.

Alternatively, this move can be used again within 1.5s while the leek-leaf shield is mid-flight causing the user to leap to where the shield is and slam it into the ground dealing damage in an area, applying a 70% slow for 3s, and granting the user a shield for 4s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Improves the strength of the shield.

Upgrade 2.Name: Detect
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user ready the leek-leaf shield while the move button is held down, for 0.6s the user is Impervious, negating damage received, afterwards the user instead reduces damage received by 30% for up to 2.4s. When the move button is released the user dashes in the designated direction dealing damage.

If the user blocks an attack, the second stage of the move will apply a 50% slow for 2.5s, and grant a shield for 4s. If an attack is blocked while the user is Impervious, the cooldowns for Fury Cutter and Leaf Blade are reduced by 50% of their max cooldown.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this moves cooldown by 1s.

Unite Move.Name: Lunging Leek Nova Blast
Unite Move.Description:
The user becomes Unstoppable,  as well as maxing out the Steadfast gauge, and charges in the designated direction, shoving any opposing Pokémon along the way for up to 1s, dealing damage to them up to 5 times. If this move does damage near the end of the dash an explosion occurs dealing double damage and stunning opponents in the area for 0.4s.

This move can go through walls if there is enough distance left.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Torrent
Passive Ability.Description: While HP is at 50% or less, Attack and movement speed are increased.

Greninja.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack. At long range, a boosted attack deals increased damage to enemies and decreases their movement speed for a short time. At close range, a boosted attack deals more damage the lower the enemies remaining HP is.

Auto attacks used in close range instead deal a bonus 5% missing HP damage to opposing Pokémon hit. Boosted attacks used in close range dash through the target, dealing a bonus 10% missing HP damage to opposing Pokémon hit instead. Against wild Pokémon: the total damage of auto attacks used in close range is capped at 500.

Move 1.Name: Bubble
Move 1.Description: Attack with a spray of bubbles, decreasing the movement speed of enemies when it hits.

Upgrade 1.Name: Water Shuriken
Upgrade 1.Description:
Throw shurikens made of water in quick succession. Decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time and restores the user's HP when it hits. Also increases the user's movement speed while using this move.

Increases the number of shurikens thrown.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Throws 5 shurikens.

Upgrade 2.Name: Surf
Upgrade 2.Description:
Ride a wave, dealing damage to enemies and restoring the user's HP. If this move knocks out an enemy, its cooldown is reset. In addition, this move deals increased damage to enemies with low HP.

Increases the amount of HP restored.

Against wild Pokémon: the damage of the wave is capped at 1200; bonus damage is capped at 600.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the HP restored by this move.

Move 2.Name: Substitute
Move 2.Description:
Leaves behind a substitute doll for the user while it's moving forward.

The Substitute doll has the same HP, Defense and Sp. Def as the user; the Substitute doll receives 500% of the damage the user would take.

Upgrade 1.Name: Double Team
Upgrade 1.Description:
Quickly moves the user away from the current position, creating illusory copies of it to aid in battle.

Reduced cooldown.

The copies have the same HP, Defense and Sp. Def as Greninja when created, but receive 500% of the damage they would otherwise take. The clones attack nearby opposing Pokémon with Bubble, Water Shuriken, or Surf (whichever move the user has learned), and auto attacks; but deal 19.44% of the damage they would otherwise deal.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Smokescreen
Upgrade 2.Description:
Creates a smokescreen and has the user roll away from its current location. Immediately afterward, the user begins sneaking and has its movement speed increased for a short time. This also makes its next basic attack a boosted attack.

Increased attack for a short time when the user stops sneaking.

Opposing Pokémon in the cloud of smoke have their field of vision reduced by 60%.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases Attack by 20% for 2s when exiting stealth.

Unite Move.Name: Waterburst Shuriken
Unite Move.Description:
Throw a giant water shuriken toward the designated area, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed for a short time when this move hits. The water shuriken then immediately explodes, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and throws them. Once this move ends, the user may jump in a designated direction.

Leaps off the burst water shuriken and becomes vulnerable to hindrances again as they jump down to the ground in the direction of their choosing.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 35% Attack Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Flame Body
Passive Ability.Description:
Whenever the user deals damage with a Basic Attack, Boosted Attack, Wing Attack, or Sky Attack, the target is left with a burn and a burn counter that caps at 5 stacks. The burn and burn counter lasts 4s and deals damage every 0.5s.

If Incinerate or Heat Wave are used, the burn is cleansed and the burn counter is consumed, increasing the damage of those moves by 10% per burn counter stack.

Moltres.Attack: Moltres strikes opponents with their beak. Becomes a boosted attack every fourth attack, swiping with their flaming wing. Once a boosted attack hits a target, the user gains one free move use for 3s. After this move is used or the duration expires, Moltres gains 600 unite energy. This unite energy can be boosted by Energy Amp and other similar effects.

Move 1.Name: Wing Attack
Move 1.Description: The user dashes in the designated direction, dealing damage along the path.

Upgrade 1.Name: Sky Attack
Upgrade 1.Description: The user dashes in the designated direction, dealing damage along the path. Each hit increases the user's Def and SpDef by 8% for 4s, stacking up to 5 times.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Grants a shield on a successful hit for 3s.

Move 2.Name: Incinerate
Move 2.Description: The user hurls a fiery orb in the designated direction, dealing damage to all opponents hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Heat Wave
Upgrade 1.Description: The user exhales hot breath in the designated direction, dealing damage and granting health regeneration. This health regeneration lasts 2s and ticks every 0.5s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Lowers the cooldown of this move by 1s for each Pokémon from the opposing team hit.

Unite Move.Name: Firebird Inferno
Unite Move.Description:
The user becomes Unstoppable and soars upwards before unleashing a blazing orb towards the designated area, dealing damage, applying 5 ticks of burn damage instantly, and 5 stacks of burn counter to all enemies hit.

For 20s afterwards, a modest area around the opposing team's goal zones are revealed. Moltres cannot gain unite energy from KO'ing wild Pokémon or scoring.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Cheek Pouch
Passive Ability.Description:
When Greedent eats a berry, recover HP after receiving the effects of the berry. At the same time, Greedent stashes another berry in its tail. Being hit by a single attack or combination of attacks will cause a berry to drop from Greedent's tail.

Oran berry or unite move berry heals Greedent for 1% Max HP + 3x(Lv-1) + 23; Sitrus or Salac berry heals Greedent for 1% Max HP + 0x(Lv-1) + 23. All other players heal from picking up an Oran berry for 2% Max HP + 5x(Level-1) + 45 (level and Max HP scaling is based on the player that ate the Oran berry).

Greedent.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits and causing one berry to drop from Greedent's tail.

Move 1.Name: Tackle
Move 1.Description: Leap to the designated area, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decrease their movement speed for a short time. If this move is used while Covet is active, no area can be designated for the user to leap to. Instead, while the user is running, damage is dealt in an area to enemies in front of Greedent. If Greedent has any berries stashed, consume all of them, reducing Tackle's cooldown for each berry consumed.

Upgrade 1.Name: Bullet Seed
Upgrade 1.Description:
Spit berry seeds in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decrease their movement speed for a short time. The more berries Greedent has stashed, the more damage this move deals.

Increased damage.

Fires 1 wave of seeds every 0.8s while held down (up to 7.5 waves). Launches 1 seed every 0.1s per wave; fires a minimum of 3 seeds, plus 1 additional seed per Berry stored before a Berry is consumed (up to 5 additional seeds; maximum 8 seeds total per wave).
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increased damage.

Upgrade 2.Name: Belch
Upgrade 2.Description:
Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decrease their movement speed for a short time. If Greedent has any berries stashed, consume all of them, reducing Belch's cooldown for each berry consumed.

Increased damage.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increased damage.

Move 2.Name: Defense Curl
Move 2.Description: Grants Greedent a shield for a short time. In addition, a berry will drop in front of Greedent.

Upgrade 1.Name: Stuff Cheeks
Upgrade 1.Description:
Greedent drops several berries, which can be eaten to recover HP. Any HP recovered that exceeds Greedent's max HP will be converted to a shield. If Greedent catches sight of a berry while using this move, movement speed is increased for a short time.

Increase the number of berries dropped.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increase the number of Oran berries dropped by 1.

Upgrade 2.Name: Covet
Upgrade 2.Description:
Greedent runs with all its might, becoming unstoppable and dealing damage to enemies hit and throwing them. While running, Greedent drops berries. Eating a dropped berry increases movement speed for a short time. The more berries eaten, the more movement speed is increased. In addition, Belch or Bullet Seed can also be used while running.

Increased damage dealt and increased frequency of berries dropping.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increased damage and increased frequency of Oran berries dropping (8 berries over 6s).

Unite Move.Name: Berry Belly Flop
Unite Move.Description: Greedent eats a berry to recover HP and then leaps to the designated area, dealing damage and throwing enemies within the area of effect. Greedent then eats a special berry, allowing an unlimited amount of berries to be stored for a short time and resets cooldowns of all moves.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 40% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Blaze
Passive Ability.Description: Increases Attack by 20% for 5 seconds after Charizard gets hit.

Mega Charizard Y.Attack: Breathes fire at opposing Pokémon, dealing damage 4 times per basic attack. Charizard may move while attacking but is slowed by 5% while breathing fire. Charizard deals additional damage to opposing Pokémon that have been burned by the user's moves. This additional damage cannot land critical hits; additional damage benefits from regular lifesteal. Basic attacks gain increased range upon evolving into Charmeleon and into Charizard.

Move 1.Name: Flame Burst
Move 1.Description:
The user attacks with a flame in the designated direction which bursts on the first opposing Pokémon hit or after traveling max distance, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and burning them for 4s (dealing damage 5 times). When this move hits, the user's movement speed is increased by 20% for 3s.

The Attack of opposing Pokémon burned by this move is decreased by 5%.

Upgrade 1.Name: Flamethrower
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user attacks with a blast of fire in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in a line and burning them for 4s (dealing damage 5 times). When this move hits, the user's movement speed is increased by 40% for 3s. The user is able to move freely when using this move. If the user is mega evolved, the user's movement speed is instead increased by 10% for 3s.

The Attack of opposing Pokémon burned by this move is decreased by 5%.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move and its burns.

Move 2.Name: Fire Spin
Move 2.Description: The user shoots fire in the designated direction which bursts into a vortex of fire on the first opposing Pokémon hit or after traveling max distance, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. The vortex of fire remains for 5s, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect every 0.5s (up to 10 times) and decreasing their movement speed by 25% for 1s when hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Fire Blast
Upgrade 1.Description: The user shoots an intense blast of fire in the designated direction which explodes on the first opposing Pokémon hit or after traveling max distance, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. The blast of all-consuming fire remains on the ground for 5s, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect every 0.5s (up to 10 times) and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 1s when hit. The user is able to move freely when using this move. If the user is mega evolved, the area of effect of this move increases.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move and the damage over time.

Unite Move.Name: Seismic Slam
Unite Move.Description:
The user briefly becomes unstoppable and grabs the designated Pokémon from the opposing team. The target is bound, and the user and the target become invincible as the user flies up then slams them onto the ground, damaging the target and nearby opposing Pokémon, dealing an additional 8% max HP damage to each opposing Pokémon hit. The user gains hindrance resistance and mega evolves into Mega Charizard Y the moment they slam into the ground and fly up into the air for 15s. While flying in the air, the user is able to use basic attacks as well as moves and is able to move freely over obstacles. Unite Move basic attacks burn opposing Pokémon for 1s (dealing damage 2 times). The user restores HP for 80% of the damage dealt with Unite Move basic attacks (excluding burns or additional damage). Damage dealt by burns from Unite Move basic attacks also benefit from regular lifesteal.

The Attack of opposing Pokémon burned by this move is decreased by 5%.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 10% Movement Speed, 10% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Static
Passive Ability.Description:
When the user receives damage from nearby Pokémon from the opposing team, the damaging Pokémon becomes paralyzed. This Ability goes on a 15s cooldown when triggered. When the user hits with a boosted attack, Thunder Shock, or Thunderbolt, they create an electric field. This field will remain for 3s, dealing damage and slowing opposing Pokémon in it by 30%.

The movement speed of opposing Pokémon paralyzed by this Ability is decreased by 30% and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 2.5s.

Zapdos.Attack: Gains a Boosted Attack every 8s, launching a powerful electric orb and dealing damage to opposing Pokémon. If this move hits Pokémon from the opposing team, the user's boosted attack cooldowns are reduced.

Move 1.Name: Thunder Shock
Move 1.Description: Attack with a jolt of electricity. If this move hits an opposing Pokémon, it also zaps a nearby opposing Pokémon, dealing damage to them.

Upgrade 1.Name: Thunderbolt
Upgrade 1.Description:
Unleash a strong electric blast to the designated area, dealing damage and paralyzing opposing Pokémon.

The movement speed of opposing Pokémon paralyzed by this Ability is decreased by 30% and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 2.5s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 6% of the current cooldown for each Pokémon hit.

Move 2.Name: Discharge
Move 2.Description:
Has the user let loose 2 waves of electricity from their feathers, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and leaving them paralyzed. If there is an electric field within this move's area of effect, the electric field is absorbed, increasing this move's area of effect and releasing a third wave of electricity. The second and third waves deal a stacking 15% increased damage to Pokémon hit by the preceeding waves.

The movement speed of opposing Pokémon paralyzed by this Ability is decreased by 30% and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 2.5s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Zap Cannon
Upgrade 1.Description: Fire an electric cannon-like blast that explodes on contact, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon caught in the explosion's area of effect. When the electric orb passes through an electric field, it absorbs the field, increasing its size, travel distance, and damage dealt. Opposing Pokémon caught in the explosion are also left unable to act for 0.3s. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve for this move, and there is a 1s cooldown between uses.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: When the electric orb passes through an electric field, the orb's size increases, along with the duration opposing Pokémon are left unable to act.

Unite Move.Name: High-Voltage Siege
Unite Move.Description:
Has the user continuously drop lightning bolts in the designated area every 1s, creating an electric field where the lightning strikes. The lightning bolts paralyze and deal damage to opposing Pokémon they hit. If lightning bolts hit the same Pokémon 3 consecutive times, that Pokémon will be left unable to act. If lightning bolts hit one of the opposing team's goal zones 5 times, that goal zone will become defenseless (scoring is instant) for 5s or until a goal is scored.

The movement speed of opposing Pokémon paralyzed by this Ability is decreased by 30% and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 2.5s.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Justified
Passive Ability.Description: When the user is damaged, their attack stat increases by 8% for 4s up to 4 times.

Mega Lucario.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with increased range every third attack, hitting twice. The first hit damages opposing Pokémon with a normal basic attack; the second hit is a follow-up attack with a bone, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area a second time and throwing them for 0.5s.

Move 1.Name: Quick Attack
Move 1.Description: Dashes forward, damaging opposing Pokémon hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Power-Up Punch
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user receives 30% reduced damage and decreases their movement speed by 15% as they charge up power for up to 4s, reaching maximum charge after 1s; then dashes forward with a devastating punch when released, damaging the first opposing Pokémon hit, dealing additional missing HP damage to the target and shoving them for 0.5s. If this move hits, the user readies a boosted attack. This move's cooldown is reduced by 1s each time one of the user's moves lands a hit. If this move is canceled or not released after 4s, it goes on a 1.4s cooldown.

When the user is mega evolved; the move can hit multiple opposing Pokémon and can be overcharged for an additional 0.4s to throw all opposing Pokémon it hits for 1s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Becomes unstoppable while charging this move.

Move 2.Name: Meteor Mash
Move 2.Description: Punches with the force of a meteor in the designated direction after 0.4s, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and shoving them in the designated direction, stunning those hit for 0.4s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Close Combat
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user becomes unstoppable and rapidly punches in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area in front of them (up to 11 times), and restoring the user's HP each time this move hits. The next auto attack within 5s after using this move gains increased range and causes the user to lunge at an opposing Pokémon with a kick.

When the user is mega evolved; the move lasts longer dealing damage up to 16 times reducing this move's cooldown by 0.4s for each hit, can be slowly rotated, the last hit can be targeted in any direction, and this move can be ended early, after channeling for at least 1s, by activating the move again or by using Power-Up Punch. The first hit of this move has 12x the normal healing. Hitting an enemy with Power-Up punch reduces the cooldown of this move by 1s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Unite Move.Name: Aura Cannon
Unite Move.Description:
The user becomes unstoppable and channels aura into a huge sphere, then launches the sphere in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in its path. The aura sphere picks up speed as it travels; at close range, a target can be hit up to 4 times, while at max speed a target will only be hit once.

After unleashing this move the user mega evolves for 25s adding additional effects to Close Combat and Power-Up Punch and changing the passive ability to Adaptability.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Pastel Veil
Passive Ability.Description:
The Pokémon's psychic counter charges based on the distance they run (up to 10 times). When the counter is full, the Pokémon is granted a shield, becoming hindrance resistant while the shield remains.

When the Pokémon reaches an additional movement speed threshold, the damage dealt by moves is increased by 5/20/35/50/60%. The increased damage based on movement speed is calculated before dealing damage with moves.

Galarian Rapidash.Attack: Shoots energy from their horn, damaging the opposing Pokémon hit.

Move 1.Name: Confusion
Move 1.Description: The user releases a telekinetic force around themself, increasing their movement speed by 33% per opposing Pokémon hit for 5.5s (up to 2 times; maximum 66%) before damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect when it hits.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dazzling Gleam
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user emits a powerful flash around themself after a delay, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect when it hits. The user gains 1 stack of 11% increased movement speed per wild Pokémon hit by the outer edge of the flash, or 2 stacks per Pokémon from the opposing team hit for 5.5s (up to 9 stacks; maximum 99% increased movement speed) before damaging them. If the outer edge of the flash hits Pokémon from the opposing team, the cooldown of Tackle, Smart Strike, or Agility is reduced by 30% of its current cooldown, successively with each target hit.

Against wild Pokémon: Dazzling Gleam damage is capped at 2500.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the cooldown reduction of Smart Strike or Agility to 50% of its current cooldown successively per Pokémon from the opposing team hit by the outer edge.

Upgrade 2.Name: Fairy Wind
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user jumps to the designated location then stirs up a fairy wind when they land, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. This move can be used again, up to 2 times total.

If it's used 2 times, the third attack deals increased damage and throws opposing Pokémon for 0.8s when it hits.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Decreases the movement speed of opposing Pokémon hit by 20% for 2s with each use.

Move 2.Name: Tackle
Move 2.Description: The user charges at the designated opposing Pokémon, damaging them and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s when it hits.

Upgrade 1.Name: Smart Strike
Upgrade 1.Description: The user charges at the designated opposing Pokémon and stabs them with their sharp horn, dealing damage when it hits. The user then leaps in a chosen direction, shoving the target in the opposite direction and stunning them for 1s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Charges the user's psychic counter by 5 when this move hits.

Upgrade 2.Name: Agility
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user's movement speed increases by 20% when this move is used, increasing by an additional 7.5% every 0.5s over 3s (maximum 80% increased movement speed).

This move's duration increases by 1.5s if the user moves or hits opposing Pokémon with moves (up to 5 times for an additional 7.5s; maximum 10.5s duration).
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Also restores 2% of the user's missing HP every 0.5s while their movement speed is increased by this move.

Unite Move.Name: Triad Blitz
Unite Move.Description:
The user becomes unstoppable and charges at the designated opposing Pokémon, shoving opposing Pokémon in the way and stunning them for 0.8s when hit. When the charge ends, a mysterious triangular space is created around the designated opposing Pokémon if the user hits them. That Pokémon cannot leave that space.

When the mysterious space is created, this move can be used again up to 3 times within 5.5s. With each additional use, the user runs at high speed forward, damaging opposing Pokémon in their path. If the path of a dash starts or ends in the mysterious space, this move can be used again immediately afterward.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Run Away
Passive Ability.Description: Restores the Pokémon's HP and nullifies damage from Pokémon from the opposing team. This Ability goes on a 30s cooldown after it's triggered.
Passive Ability.Description 2: Grants a water barrier after 30s. If the Pokémon takes damage from the opposing team while the water barrier is active, the shield nullifies the damage and restores the Pokémon's HP instead (30s cd). Hitting a Pokémon from the opposing team with a move will reduce this Ability's cooldown by 2s, and by 1s with a boosted attack

Vaporeon.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

Move 1.Name: Swift
Move 1.Description:
Has the user shoots 6 star-shaped rays in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit and reducing their movement speed by 35% for 0.3s. The user is slowed by 30% while casting this move.

The slow duration is extended by 0.3s for each star that hits the same Pokémon.

Upgrade 1.Name: Hydro Pump
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user continuously launch a huge volume of pressurized water in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit and shoves them for 0.1s. The move ends by unleashing a powerful blast of water, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit and shoves them for 1s. The user is slowed by 30% while casting this move.

This move slows opposing Pokémon hit by 50% for 0.3s, further extended with each hit.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Muddy Water
Upgrade 2.Description: Has the user splash muddy water around itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon, slowing them by 30% for 1s, and throwing them for 0.6s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the duration of the slow by 1s.

Move 2.Name: Tackle
Move 2.Description:
Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit and shoves them for 0.25s.

The distance this move travels is cut short when colliding into opposing Pokémon.

Upgrade 1.Name: Aqua Ring
Upgrade 1.Description: Vaporeon jumps in a designated direction, creating continuous waves centered around herself for 2.25s. Vaporeon and any ally Pokémon that come in contact with a wave gains a buff that lasts 1.5s, healing once every 0.5s. This buff on an ally can be refreshed every time they come in contact with a wave, healing up to a maximum of 7 times.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the amount of HP restored.

Upgrade 2.Name: Flip Turn
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user cloak itself in water for up to 3s, reducing damage received by 15% and increasing movement speed by 40%, ramping up by 5% every 0.25s up to 60%. This move can be used again within 3s to charge in a designated direction, dealing damage and shoving opposing Pokémon for 0.6s before jumping back in the opposite direction and gaining a shield.

Vaporeon cannot auto attack or cast other moves whiile cloaked in water.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage reduction to 30%.

Unite Move.Name: Aquamarine Splash
Unite Move.Description: Has the user jump up high and dive underwater, creating a current that pulls in nearby opposing Pokémon for 0.3s. Afterward, the user leaps out of the water, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and throws them for 1s.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 40% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Swift Swim
Passive Ability.Description:
When damaged by a Pokémon from the opposing team; increases movement speed by 40% for 2s, decaying by 10% every 0.5s (minimum 25% increased movement speed) (10s CD).

This Pokémon's headache gauge increases by 1% for every 0.5% max HP damage received by Psyduck or nearby ally Pokémon from Pokémon on the opposing team. This gauge fills at a rate of 30% per 1s (requiring a total of 3.33s to fully charge). When the headache gauge is full, a mysterious power is triggered, interrupting Psyduck from casting moves, cleansing status conditions on themself, becoming unstoppable and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 1s; then shoving nearby opposing Pokémon away for 0.8s, and resetting this Pokémon's move cooldowns. The mysterious power stays active for 10s, boosting moves and giving them 20% cooldown reduction. When the mysterious power ends, Psyduck cannot use moves or attacks, or charge their headache gauge for 2.5s.

Psyduck.Attack:
Attacks with a shot of water.

Psyduck has no boosted attack.

Move 1.Name: Confusion
Move 1.Description:
Releases a weak telekinetic force in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon and stunning them for 1s when it hits.

While the mysterious power is active, deals increased damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Surf
Upgrade 1.Description:
Creates a wave in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2.5s when it hits; if opposing Pokémon already have their movement speed decreased when this move hits, they are also shoved for 0.5s. Rides the wave for at least 0.6s and until this move is used again or for up to 5s, moving forward and increasing movement speed by 30%; the user can use basic attacks and moves while riding the wave. Ally Pokémon who touch the wave while Psyduck is riding it have their movement speed increased by 70% for 3s (decaying by 10% every 0.5s over 2s; minimum 30%). When this move is used again, stops in place and creates another wave in the designated direction.

While the mysterious power is active, creates two waves in the designated direction each time this move is used.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Decreases the damage dealt by opposing Pokémon hit by this move by 25% for 2.5s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Bubble Beam
Upgrade 2.Description:
Forcefully ejects a spray of bubbles in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and attaching a mark with 3 bubbles to those hit for 5s. When a teammate damages opposing Pokémon with bubbles attached to them with auto attacks, a bubble bursts, damaging the target. When all bubbles on a target burst from auto attack damage, they are stunned for 0.5s and their movement speed is decreased by 30% for 2s.

While the mysterious power is active, strengthens the damage dealt when bubbles burst.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Decreases the movement speed of opposing Pokémon hit by the beam by 30% for 2s.

Move 2.Name: Tail Whip
Move 2.Description:
Wags their tail at the designated opposing Pokémon, damaging them and decreasing their movement speed by 30% and their Sp. Def by 20% for 2s.

While the mysterious power is active, strengthens the Sp. Def decrease to 30%.

Upgrade 1.Name: Disable
Upgrade 1.Description:
Releases electricity at the designated opposing Pokémon, damaging the target and stunning them for 0.2s. Opposing Pokémon hit by the electricity from this use of the move have a mark applied to them for 5s which has a disabling effect tied to it; the disabling effect increases the cooldown of the last move used by a marked target by 2s, or the next move used if no move is on cooldown (once only). When the disabling effect occurs, the target's movement speed is also decreased by 30% for 2s. The electricity spreads to a nearby opposing Pokémon up to 4 times; the electricity can only spread to the same target up to 2 times.

While the mysterious power is active, the electricity can spread up to 6 times; the electricity can spread to the same target up to 2 times.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the stun duration to 0.5s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Psychic
Upgrade 2.Description:
Decreases movement speed by 10% and releases a strong telekinetic force in the designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and stunning them for 0.6s when it hits. Continues to release a telekinetic force at the Pokémon from the opposing team closest to the center of the area of effect when this move hits, marking them for 2s. If this move is used again or after 2s and the user is in range of the marked target, pulls the target a set distance in the designated direction (pulling them toward the user's location if no direction is designated), stunning them for 0.85s during the pull.

While the mysterious power is active, stuns the target marked by this move for 0.2s after being pulled.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Decreases the Sp. Def of opposing Pokémon hit by the area of effect by 25% for 2s.

Unite Move.Name: Full-Power Psy-ay-ay!
Unite Move.Description:
Releases three mysterious psychic waves around themself, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing the damage dealt by them by 30% for 3s when hit.

After using this Unite Move, increases the headache gauge by 50% as a consequence of Psyduck unleashing their full power.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Rough Skin
Passive Ability.Description: When struck by a melee attack, a portion of the damage received is reflected back at the attacker.

Garchomp.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every fifth attack, dealing increased damage and restoring the user's HP. Every time a basic attack or move hits an enemy, basic attack speed is increased.

Against wild Pokémon: basic attack damage is capped at 1000; boosted attack damage is capped at 1100; additional remaining HP damage is capped at 350.

Move 1.Name: Sand Attack
Move 1.Description: Hurls sand, narrowing the field of vision of enemies and decreases their movement speed for a short time. Also increases movement speed and basic attack speed.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dig
Upgrade 1.Description:
Burrow underground and move quickly in the designated direction, jumping out when coming in contact with an enemy or when traveling the maximum distance. This jump deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and throws them.

Damage received is reduced for a short time after jumping out of the ground.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the duration of the throw to 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Dragon Rush
Upgrade 2.Description:
Back up to create distance before diving to attack in the designated direction, shoving enemies. The user can use Dragon Claw while creating distance at the start of this move to create a combo attack. (This move can crit)

Decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time when they are shoved the maximum distance.

The user can use Dragon Claw during the first half of the channel to add the Dragon Claw hits as a combo attack. This combo attack scoops up opposing Pokémon, dealing damage with both Dragon Rush and Dragon Claw's first attack and stunning them. If the combo attack hits, the user then throws opposing Pokémon back down with the second Dragon Claw attack, dealing damage with a strengthened slow.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Becomes unstoppable during this move; this includes the combo attack with Dragon Claw.

Move 2.Name: Bulldoze
Move 2.Description: Stomp down on the ground, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect.

Upgrade 1.Name: Earthquake
Upgrade 1.Description:
Jump high into the air and crash forcefully to the ground, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect.

Decrease the movement speed of enemies for a short time if they are in this move's area of effect.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the movement speed decrease of opposing Pokémon near the center of the crash to 40%.

Upgrade 2.Name: Dragon Claw
Upgrade 2.Description:
Attack twice in front of itself using its claws. The first attack deals damage to enemies and throws them. The second attack deals increased damage. After the move ends, basic attack speed is increased for a short time. (This move can crit)

Increase damage dealt.

When this move is used in a combo attack with Dragon Rush, the movement speed decrease on the second attack is strengthened to 60% for 2s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of both attacks.

Unite Move.Name: Livid Outrage
Unite Move.Description: Has the user unleash a powerful chain attack while dashing forward. The final attack throws enemies but also leaves the user unable to act for a short time.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Flame Body
Passive Ability.Description: When Charcadet hits an enemy with an auto attack, Charcadet deals damage around themself with flames. This ability goes on a 4s cd after it's triggered.

Ceruledge.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

Move 1.Name: Lava Plume
Move 1.Description: Torch the area around the user with an inferno of scarlet flames, dealing damage to enemies hit. The user's next basic attack deals 15% increased damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Bitter Blade
Upgrade 1.Description: Ceruledge focuses bitter feelings towards the world of the living into a slashing attack. The user recovers HP equals to 50% of the damage dealt with this move. Damage dealt to wild Pokémon only heals for half of the normal amount. Any leftover healing from the move that exceeds the user's max HP is converted into a shield (effects that alter healing directly are ignored). The shield lasts 2s, the duration is refreshed with each application, and can stack up to 10x. The maximum capacity of the shield is determined by the life steal amount.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the amount of HP recovered to 70% of damage dealt.

Upgrade 2.Name: Psycho Cut
Upgrade 2.Description: Ceruledge launches a blade formed by psychic power in the designated direction, dealing damage and slowing enemies hit by 30% for 3s, and reducing their Defense for 4s. If Ceruledge hits enemies with a basic attack, this move's cooldown is reduced by 0.5s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the Defense reduction of enemies hit by this move.

Move 2.Name: Take Down
Move 2.Description: Has the user charge recklessly in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. The user's basic attack speed is increased by 20% for 3s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Phantom Force
Upgrade 1.Description: Dash and disappear into darkness, where Ceruledge hides for 1s. While hiding, Ceruledge cannot move. When this move is used again or after 1s has passed, the user appears in the designated direction while slashing around themself, dealing damage that ignores Defense and shields of enemies and applies a 60% slow for 1s to them. If this move hits an enemy, this move's cooldown is reduced by 40% of the max cooldown.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Flame Charge
Upgrade 2.Description:
Ceruledge cloaks themself in flame and charges in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. Ceruledge's basic attack speed is increased by 30% for 3s after using this move. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve for this move.

There is a 1 second cooldown between each use, which is reduced with cooldown reduction.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Ceruledge's basic attack speed is instead increased for 5s after using this move.

Unite Move.Name: Revenant Rend
Unite Move.Description:
Ceruledge charges both of their arm blades gaining unstoppable and 70% Damage reduction for 0.5s before slashing through to the designated location, leaving Ceruledge unable to act for 0.7s. Ceruledge also leaves enemies hit unable to act for 0.7s and applies a mark to them. After 0.7s, marked enemies are dealt damage with three slashing blows.

The mark can be cleansed, preventing the damage.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Swift Swim
Passive Ability.Description: When the Pokémon uses a move, their movement speed increases by 30% for 3s. Each time this Pokémon knocks out an opposing Pokémon, scores a goal, or otherwise makes a contribution in battle, their effort gauge increases. Once the gauge is full, the Pokémon evolves into Gyarados.

Mega Gyarados.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

Magikarp and Mega Evolved Gyarados do not have a boosted attack. Mega Evolved Gyarados lunges when using a basic attack.

Move 1.Name: Flail
Move 1.Description: Flails on the spot, damaging nearby opposing Pokémon; damage strengthens for every 33% max HP the user is missing.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dragon Breath
Upgrade 1.Description:
Exhales a mighty gust in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and paralyzing them for 3s when it hits, decreasing their movement speed and basic attack speed by 30%. If this move hits, gains a buff for 5s; increasing the range and damage of auto attacks; and increasing basic attack speed by a minimum of 30%, strengthening by 10% for each additional opposing Pokémon hit (up to 2 times; maximum 50%).

If the user is mega evolved, instead Mega Gyarados becomes unstoppable then chases down and performs 5 special auto attacks on targets hit by this attack.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage dealt by this move and basic attacks.

Move 2.Name: Splash
Move 2.Description:
Jumps to the designated location forward.

A maximum of 3 uses can be kept in reserve, with a 2s cooldown between uses.

Upgrade 1.Name: Waterfall
Upgrade 1.Description:
Charges forward up to 3 times, damaging opposing Pokémon hit with each charge; waiting for at least 0.07s before dashing forward with the next charge. The first charge is a regular dash forward; the next movement within 2s after the last charge dashes forward with the same effect. Dragon Breath can be used within the 2s duration in between charges without interrupting this move's course. Using Dragon Current ends this move early. This move's cooldown begins when the move ends. If two charges hit opposing Pokémon, grants a shield for 2s. If all three charges hit opposing Pokémon, the third charge throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.45s.

If the user is mega evolved, instead the cooldown begins when the move is used. Additionally, the previous shield and throw effects are granted even if the move misses, and the cooldown of the move is reduced by 10% for each dash that hits.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Unite Move.Name: Dragon Current
Unite Move.Description:
Becomes untargetable and dives underwater for up to 4s while this Unite Move is held, increasing movement speed by 45%. When the button is released, the user leaps up forcefully and attacks. If the user stays underwater for 1s, Gyarados strengthens the movement speed increase to 55% and creates a whirlpool when surfacing, applying a continuous slow to opposing Pokémon hit. If the user stays underwater for 2s, Gyarados strengthens the movement speed increase to 65% and creates a waterspout when surfacing, throwing opposing Pokémon hit for 1s. The longer the move is held, the larger the area of effect becomes.

After using this Unite Move, the user Mega Evolves for 15 seconds.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Filter
Passive Ability.Description: Reduced damage received for a short time after using a move.

Mr. Mime.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage and throwing enemies when it hits.

Move 1.Name: Fake Out
Move 1.Description: Slap its hands together, shoving enemies in front of it.

Upgrade 1.Name: Confusion
Upgrade 1.Description:
Attacks straight in front of the user, dealing damage to enemies and shoving them. If an enemy shoved by this move hits an obstacle, it receives increased damage from this move and is left unable to act for a short time. If the enemy is currently guard-swapped, it is not shoved but receives increased damage from this move and is left unable to act for a short time.

Increased damage.

Normal damage will apply to targets that are unstoppable or unstunnable. (Example: Drednaw & Zapdos)
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increased damage.

Upgrade 2.Name: Psychic
Upgrade 2.Description:
Centers one telekinetic blast each on the user, any guard-swapped enemies, and any Light Screen or Barrier walls created by the user, damaging all enemies caught in the blasts and lowering their Sp. Defense for a short time. If an enemy is hit by multiple overlapping blasts, that enemy is also left unable to act for a short time.

Also decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Enemies hit by the pulses are additionally slowed by 30%.

Move 2.Name: Light Screen
Move 2.Description: Create a wall of light in the designated area that prevents enemies from passing.

Upgrade 1.Name: Barrier
Upgrade 1.Description:
Create a translucent wall in the designated location that prevents enemies from passing. A maximum of two uses can be kept in reserve for this move and cannot be used more than once every 2s.

Increases the max number of uses that can be kept in reserve.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Stores 3 uses in reserve instead.

Upgrade 2.Name: Power Swap
Upgrade 2.Description:
Swaps the user's Defense and Sp. Defense stats with those of another Pokémon for a short time when it hits. Swapping with an enemy deals damage over time and decreases their movement speed while increasing the user's movement speed for a short time. Swapping with an ally increases the movement speed of that ally and the user for a short time.

Also continually restores the HP of the user and the ally while this move is in effect.

If the linked target is an ally, this move increases their movement speed by 8%, increases the amount of damage they deal by 15%, and continually restores their HP. If the linked target is an enemy, this move decreases their movement speed by 8%, decreases the amount of damage they deal by 15% and deals damage over time to them. Power Swap can be cleansed.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the amount of damage dealt by allies to 20% and decreases the amount of damage dealt by enemies to 20%.

Unite Move.Name: Showtime
Unite Move.Description: Jump to the designated location and start a pantomime performance, dealing damage over time to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them unable to act for a short time.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Inner Focus
Passive Ability.Description: Reduces the duration of hindrances inflicted on Kubfu by 15%.
Passive Ability.Description 2: When Urshifu deals damage to an opposing Pokémon with an active shield effect, 30% of the damage dealt to the shield is dealt as additional damage to the target's HP.

Urshifu.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with increased range every third attack, lunging at an opposing Pokémon and dealing increased damage.

Move 1.Name: Rock Smash
Move 1.Description: The user receives 20% reduced damage as they charge power for up to 2s, increasing power levels until reaching maximum charge after 1s; then punches in the designated direction when released, damaging opposing Pokémon hit. If this move is canceled or not released after 2s, it goes on a 0.9s cooldown.

Upgrade 1.Name: Wicked Blow
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user becomes unstoppable, receives 20% reduced damage and decreases their movement speed by 30% as they charge power for up to 2.5s, becoming charged after 1s; then strikes in the designated direction with a fierce blow when released, readying a boosted attack if it hits. When uncharged, the cooldown of this move is reduced by 50%; and the user strikes in a small line area of effect, damaging opposing Pokémon and decreasing their movement speed when it hits.

When charged, the user strikes in a larger cone area of effect, dealing increased damage to opposing Pokémon and dealing additional missing HP damage to those hit. Opposing Pokémon in the center line area of effect receive a critical hit and are shoved. If this move is canceled or not released after 2.5s, it goes on a 2.1s cooldown.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens damage reduction while charging this move to 40%. If this move is charged when released, opposing Pokémon in the center line area of effect are also stunned with the shove.

Upgrade 2.Name: Surging Strikes
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user dashes forward and strikes with a flowing motion, damaging opposing Pokémon hit. This move can be used up to two more times within 4s of the previous use. Opposing Pokémon hit by the third use receive a critical hit and are thrown.

Each time this move is used, the user readies an enhanced boosted attack which restores the user's HP per hit.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Receives 30% reduced damage while using this move.

Move 2.Name: Headbutt
Move 2.Description: The user dashes forward with a headbutt, damaging opposing Pokémon and shoving them when it hits.

Upgrade 1.Name: Throat Chop
Upgrade 1.Description: Dash in the designated direction and strike with a flowing motion, dealing damage to enemies hit. This move can be used an additional two times and the third time it is used, any hits are always critical hits and the enemy is thrown. Urshifu's next basic attack after using this move becomes a boosted attack and if the boosted attack hits, Urshifu's HP is restored.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 40% of its remaining cooldown when Wicked Blow hits.

Upgrade 2.Name: Liquidation
Upgrade 2.Description:
Deal damage to and decrease the defense of enemies in the area around Urshifu by 30% for 2s. When this move hits, Urshifu is granted a shield whose strength is based on the number of enemies hit.

Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Unite Move.Name: Flowing Fists
Unite Move.Description:
The user activates the Flowing Fists buff for up to 10s, and briefly becomes unstoppable and damages nearby opposing Pokémon, shoving them away. If this Unite Move is used again, the user becomes unstoppable and receives 30% reduced damage as they unleash a minimum of 5 consecutive blows upon the designated Pokémon from the opposing team, dealing damage to the target and shoving them with each hit. After the consecutive blows have finished, the user delivers a final kick, damaging opposing Pokémon in front of themself and shoving them when it hits.

When the Flowing Fists buff is active, until the follow-up attack is used, every 2 times the user hits a Pokémon from the opposing team, the number of consecutive blows for the follow-up attack increases by 1 (up to 10 hits for an additional 5 blows; maximum 10 consecutive blows).
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Torrent
Passive Ability.Description: When the Pokémon is at half HP or less, its Attack and Sp. Attack are increased.

Blastoise.Attack:
Blastoise’s third auto attack is empowered - dealing 3 instances of damage, slowing enemies hit and reducing Blastoise’s move cooldowns by 1 second each.

Boosted attacks used during Rapid Spin hit 3 times over 0.5s, do not reduce the cooldowns of the user's moves, nor decrease the movement speed of opposing Pokémon hit.

Move 1.Name: Water Gun
Move 1.Description: Attacks with a shot of water, shoving enemies and decreasing their movement speed for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Hydro Pump
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user attack by blasing out a huge volume of water, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and shoving them when it hits.

Increases damage dealt by this move.

When used during Rapid Spin: Blastoise unleashes a large burst of water in a radius around themself, damaging enemies, knocking them back and stunning those hit for 0.75s. Blastoise also gains a 30% movement speed bonus for the duration of this move.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increased move damage.

Upgrade 2.Name: Water Spout
Upgrade 2.Description:
Spouts water toward the designated location, decreasing the movement speed of opposing Pokémon in the area of effect for a short time.

Increases damage dealt by this move.

When used during Rapid Spin: increases movement speed by 30%, and rapidly spouts bursts of water in different directions around themself, damaging opposing over time when held for the duration of Rapid Spin (up to a possible 17 times). The first hit of Water Spout during Rapid Spin on an opposing Pokémon decreases the target's movement speed; subsequent hits will not.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increased move damage.

Move 2.Name: Skull Bash
Move 2.Description: Fiercely rams an opposing Pokémon and leaves it unable to act.

Upgrade 1.Name: Surf
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user charge forward on a wave, shoving enemies and leaving them unable to act. The user becomes immune to hindrances while using this move and can jump in a designated direction once this move ends.

Also grants a shield when this move hits.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the strength of the shield.

Upgrade 2.Name: Rapid Spin
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user spin rapidly, changing the mechanics of its basic attacks as well as its Hydro Pump and Water Spout moves.

Also reduces the damage the user receives while using this move.

While spinning: changes the mechanics of auto attacks and Hydro Pump or Water Spout. When this move ends: comes out of their shell and readies a boosted attack. The user cannot perform any actions for 0.45s while withdrawing or coming out of their shell.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases Defense and Sp. Def while using this move.

Unite Move.Name: Hydro Typhoon
Unite Move.Description: Has the user spout water to attack while spinning, throwing enemies in a large area. This also grants the user a shield.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 40% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Regenerator
Passive Ability.Description: If this Pokémon doesn't take damage from enemy Pokémon (Wild Pokémon do not count) for 3s, it will recover 3% max HP every 2 seconds. Damage to shields will not deactivate Regenerator. Ho-oh additionally recovers 0.3% max HP every 2 seconds.

Ho-Oh.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, shooting out a sweeping flame that deals increased damage and restores HP per enemy Pokémon hit. While firing the sweeping flame Ho-Oh's movement speed is decreased by 30%.

Move 1.Name: Safeguard
Move 1.Description: Create a protective field around the user that reduces the next incoming source of damage by 50%. When this field reduces damage from an attack, the user's movement speed is increased by 40% for 3s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Sacred Fire
Upgrade 1.Description:
Shoot a mystical fireball at an area, dealing damage to non-allied Pokémon it hits, stunning them for 0.6s, and leaving them burned for 3s, reducing their Attack by 10%. Then fly up high into the air for up to 6s. The next 3 basic attacks are boosted attacks. While flying, the user is able to move freely over walls and its attack is increased by 25% and movement speed is increased by 30%. If this move is used again, the user lands cancelling the attack and movement speed bonuses.

Ho-Oh may not interact with objects on the ground such as Berries or Aeos Energy but may score on goal zones.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the Attack reduction of the burn to 20%.

Upgrade 2.Name: Fire Spin
Upgrade 2.Description:
Generate a blazing vortex around yourself and gain a shield. While the vortex is active, Ho-Oh slows itself by 40% and cannot attack. The first time an enemy interacts with the vortex, they receive an 80% movement speed decrease decaying by 10% every 0.5s over 4s. The vortex also deals damage to enemies. If allies are within the vortex, 60% of post-mitigation damage those allies would take is redirected to Ho-Oh as true damage (without ignoring shields).

If there is a shield after 4s or if this move is used again while this move's shield is intact, a heat wave is released around the user, dealing damage to enemies, and additional damage equal to the percentage of the damage the shield received. The emitted heat wave additionally stuns non-allied Pokémon it hits for 0.6s,  There is a 1.5s delay before the shield may be detonated from the start of the vortex. If the shield is destroyed before detonation, no damage is dealt.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: When the shield is broken, the heat wave is now automatically released.

Move 2.Name: Tailwind
Move 2.Description: Whip up a whirlwind in designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit. The whirlwind then remains on the field for 5s. Allies moving in the same direction as the whirlwind have their movement speed increased by 30% as long as they are in the field and lasting for 0.5s after stepping out of the field. Opposing Pokémon moving against the whirlwind have their movement speed decreased by 30% lasting for 0.5s after stepping out of the field.

Upgrade 1.Name: Flamethrower
Upgrade 1.Description:
Blow flames in a designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit every 0.25s and slowing Ho-Oh's movement speed by 15%. This move may be held for up to 4s to blow flames for longer. This move's cooldown increases the longer flames are blown for. If enemies are hit, the user recovers HP. If enemies are hit thrice, they are burned, refreshing on additional hits of flamethrower.

Burned enemies take damage over 3s at 0.5s intervals. Burned enemies also have their movement speed decreased by 15% for 1.5s, their attack decreased by 20% for 2s, and their special attack decreased by 20% for 1.5s. Enemies burned by Flamethrower will leave behind a scorched area. Enemies traversing the scorched area are slowed by 15%. Enemies that touch the area will also receive burns but this burn will not spread the scorched area.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the length of time flames may be blown to 5s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Sky Attack
Upgrade 2.Description:
Ho-Oh spreads their wings and charges in a loop. While charging, deal damage to enemies hit and burn the ground along the flight path for 4.5s. The burning ground deals damage to enemies that touch it every 0.5s and slows them by 30% for 1.5s. If this move is used again or if another move is used mid-charge, then the user stops in place.

During the flight, Ho-Oh receives 30% less damage.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: While the Ho-Oh is on burning ground, gradually restore HP.

Unite Move.Name: Rekindling Flame
Unite Move.Description: When a nearby ally is knocked out, a feather is left in their place until they respawn at base. When Rekindling Flame is used, Ho-oh spreads their wings and consumes all of the Aeos energy held to revive knocked-out allies where their feathers are for 60% of their maximum HP. Rekindling Flame may only be used if there are feathers nearby. The number of available revivals are based on how much Aeos energy is consumed. One ally may be always revived. Up to two allies are revivable if at least 9 energy is consumed. Up to three allies are revivable if at least 17 Aeos energy is consumed. Up to four allies are revivable if at least 25 Aeos energy is consumed.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 80% Movement Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Levitate
Passive Ability.Description:
After using a move: increases movement speed by 50% for 2s (decaying by 5% every 0.25s).

Increases movement speed by 10% while near an ally Latios. When not in combat, this Ability can be used to fly to an allied Latios. This Ability goes on cooldown for 90s after it's triggered for both Latios and Latias. While channeling this ability the user can move around, once the 2s channel is completed the user becomes unstoppable and flies to where Latios was when this ability was activated. If Latios is at the home base, the cooldown of Levitate goes down twice as fast for Latias.

Latias.Attack:
Shoots a psychic projectile at an opposing Pokémon, damaging them.

Latias has no boosted attack.

Move 1.Name: Confusion
Move 1.Description: Releases a weak telekinetic force in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Mist Ball
Upgrade 1.Description:
Releases a mist-like flurry of down in the designated direction. When used again or after 0.9s pass: the flurry of down bursts, creating a zone of mist in the area of effect dealing damage up to 2 more times. This move then goes on cooldown. The projectile and area of effect damages opposing Pokémon in it, decreases their movement speed by 40% for 2s, and marks them for 2s.

Marked opposing Pokémon deal 25% decreased damage. If either Latias or Latios deals damage to marked opposing Pokémon: increases the duration of the mark by 1s. This duration extension can be continued indefinitely.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increased mark base duration to 3s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Dragon Cheer
Upgrade 2.Description:
Rallies the designated ally Pokémon for 3s. This move can be recast within 3s to rally a second designated ally Pokémon. Alternatively the user can target themselves, however the recast must be used on an allied Pokémon. The user and the rallied Pokémon gain a shield and the rallied Pokémon deals increased damage with their next hit. If the designated ally is Latios, then Latios will additionally gain 20% movement speed for 2s.

When this move is recast; Latias will fly towards the designated ally. This move's cooldown begins once the recast is used or expires. The damage increase is based on the rallied Pokémon's level.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increased the strength of the shield.

Move 2.Name: Swift
Move 2.Description: Shoots star-shaped rays in a large area in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dragon Pulse
Upgrade 1.Description:
After learning this move: Latias gains 2 Eon power for knocking out a wild Pokémon; or 2 Eon power per Pokémon from the opposing team hit by this move. When this move is used: Latias generates a beam in the designated direction, sweeping in a cone shape, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and restoring the user's HP once. While firing the beam, Latias moves 60% slower.

This move gains 2 telekinetic projectiles at 25 and 100 Eon power, and gains 1 projectile at 50 and 75 Eon power for a total of 6 projectiles. Subsequent projectiles hitting the same target deal 15% less damage; further decreasing with each projectile to a maximum of 75% less damage. For every 1 Eon power accumulated beyond 100; the projectile damage increases by 0.5% (Eon power caps at 1099).
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: The beam now slows opposing Pokémon by 30% for 2s

Upgrade 2.Name: Dragon Breath
Upgrade 2.Description:
After learning this move: Latias gains 2 Eon power for knocking out a wild Pokémon; or 3 Eon power per Pokémon from the opposing team hit by this move. When this move is used: Latias exhales a mighty gust in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit, slowing them by 30% for 3s and decreasing their Sp. Def by 30% for 3s.

This move gains increased range at 31 and 61 Eon Power. For every 1 Eon power accumulated beyond 60; the damage increases by 0.5% (Eon power caps at 1059).
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: The slow is increased to 40% for 3s

Unite Move.Name: Mist Blast
Unite Move.Description:
Aims at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team and charges power for 1.8s before releasing a pulse, damaging all opposing Pokémon hit. Resets move cooldowns immediately after releasing the pulse, and increases cooldown reduction by 20% for 9.5s.

If Latios is also aiming its Unite Move at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team, the pulses stack and explode, dealing damage around the target as well. If Mist Blast hits last then the explosion uses Latias' stats.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Gulp Missile
Passive Ability.Description:
When Cramorant uses a move, it catches some form of prey in its mouth. Afterward, when Cramorant receives damage, it spits the prey out as a counterattack at an enemy. The type of prey and effect differ based on the user's HP.

When above 50% max HP, catches Arrokuda as prey, which also decrease opposing Pokémon's Defense by 20% and Sp. Def by 5% for 3s. When below 50% max HP, instead catches Pikachu as prey, which decrease opposing Pokémon's movement speed by 50% and attack speed by 10% for 1s.

Cramorant.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every fourth attack. This boosted attack spits Arrokuda or other Pokémon at the enemy and deals area damage near the target when it hits.

Move 1.Name: Whirlpool
Move 1.Description:
Creates a whirlpool that deals damage to enemies in the area of effect. The area of effect slowly shrinks, but the damage dealt slowly increases. Just before the whirlpool disappears, it decreases the movement speed of enemies in the area of effect for a short time. The user can also catch Arrokuda or other prey by entering the whirlpool.

The user can enter the whirlpool to catch prey for Gulp Missile (up to 2 times).

Upgrade 1.Name: Surf
Upgrade 1.Description:
Attacks with a wave that deals damage to enemies. Once the wave has traveled its full distance, it returns the way it came, dealing damage to enemies once again and pulling them back with the wave. The user can also catch Arrokuda or other prey by entering the wave.

When the first wave hits enemies, it also decreases their movement speed for a short time.

The user catches prey with the first wave, and can enter the returning wave to catch more prey for Gulp Missile.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: The first wave decreases the movement speed of opposing Pokémon hit by 40% for 3s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Dive
Upgrade 2.Description:
Changes the designated area into a puddle and has the user dive to that area, dealing damage to enemies and shoving them when they're hit. The user also catches Arrokuda or other wild Pokémon as they emerge from the puddle. A maximum of 3 uses can be kept in reserve for this move.

Increased damage.

The user catches prey for Gulp Missile as they emerge from the puddle.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of the dive.

Move 2.Name: Feather Dance
Move 2.Description: Decreases the Attack and movement speed of enemies for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Hurricane
Upgrade 1.Description:
Creates a fierce wind, dealing damage to enemies that get caught in it and throws them.

Increases the user's movement speed for a short time.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the user's movement speed by 20% for 3s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Air Slash
Upgrade 2.Description:
Shoot out many blades of air while moving backward to create distance. Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and reduces this move's cooldown when it hits.

Restores the user's HP every time a blade of air hits an enemy.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Restores the user's HP for each blade of air that hits an opposing Pokémon.

Unite Move.Name: Gatling Gulp Missile
Unite Move.Description:
Creates a puddle at the user's current location. The user continuously uses its mouth to grab Arrokuda from the puddle and spits them out to attack the nearest enemy. The user cannot move or use other moves while this move is in effect.

If a boosted attack is readied when this Unite Move is used, the user spits this prey out first as a regular boosted attack before catching and spitting out up to 10 Gatling Gulp Missile prey.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Max HP Shield granted at the start of the Move, 30% Movement Speed granted at the end of the turret

Passive Ability.Name: Levitate
Passive Ability.Description: Increases movement speed by 10% when out of combat. (5s after leaving combat)

Gengar.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, lunging at an enemy and dealing damage.

Move 1.Name: Will-o-Wisp
Move 1.Description:
Shoot multiple sinister flames, dealing damage to enemies and leaving them burned when it hits.

The burn deals damage over time every 0.5s for 2s (dealing damage 3 times), and decreases Attack and Sp. Atk by 10% for 4s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Shadow Ball
Upgrade 1.Description:
Hurl a shadowy blob at the target, decreasing the movement speed and Sp. Defense of enemies for a short time when it hits.

Increased damage.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Sludge Bomb
Upgrade 2.Description:
Hurl unsanitary sludge to the designated area, leaving enemies poisoned when it hits.

Increased poison duration.

Against wild Pokémon: poison damage is capped at 120 per tick.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Strengthens poison duration to 6s (dealing maximum 12% max HP Sp. Atk-based damage).

Move 2.Name: Lick
Move 2.Description:
Attacks with the user's tongue and pulls enemies toward the user.

The user readies a boosted attack when this move is used.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dream Eater
Upgrade 1.Description:
Puts enemies to sleep when it hits. If used again while a nearby enemy is asleep, the user appears behind the target and attacks, restoring HP and reducing the cooldown of Shadow Ball or Sludge Bomb.

Increased damage and HP restored.

The user readies a boosted attack each time this move is used. If the user knocks out a Pokémon from the opposing team, the cooldown of this move is reset.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage dealt and HP restored by this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Hex
Upgrade 2.Description:
Disappear and move to the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect when the user reappears. If this hits an enemy that is afflicted by a status condition, it deals increased damage and its cooldown is reduced.

Increased damage.

The user readies a boosted attack when this move is used.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Unite Move.Name: Phantom Ambush
Unite Move.Description:
Jump to the designated area and start sneaking with increased movement speed. If this move is used again, it deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for a short time.

The user readies a boosted attack each time this move is used.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 80% Movement Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Blaze
Passive Ability.Description:
When using a move, the next basic attack becomes a boosted attack. When hitting an enemy with a basic attack or move, a cinder is placed on that enemy. Attacking a cindered enemy a certain number of times will cause the cinder to flare, dealing damage to the enemy. When at half HP or less, critical-hit rate is increased by 10% and basic attack speed is increased by 20% for a short time.

When hitting an opposing Pokémon with an auto attack or move, places a cinder on the target for 5s; applies 2 cinders with boosted attacks and moves. When placing a 5th cinder on a target, consumes the cinders, dealing additional damage to the target.

Cinderace.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits.

Against wild Pokémon: basic attack damage is capped at 1000; boosted attack damage is capped at 1300.

Move 1.Name: Ember
Move 1.Description:
Shoot out a small flame, dealing damage to enemies, leaving them burned and decreasing their Attack for a short time.

The Attack and Sp. Atk of the burned target are decreased by 5% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Pyro Ball
Upgrade 1.Description:
Kick a ball of flame in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies and leaving them burned for a short time when this move hits.

Reduces the cooldown of Flame Charge or Feint when this move hits.

The Attack and Sp. Atk of burned opposing Pokémon are decreased by 5% for 2s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces the cooldown of Flame Charge or Feint by 4s when this move hits.

Upgrade 2.Name: Blaze Kick
Upgrade 2.Description:
Perform a fiery flying kick. This move is always a critical hit, and it shoves enemies and leaves them burned for a short time. In addition, become immune to hindrances while using this move.

Increases basic attack speed for a short time when this move hits.

The Attack and Sp. Atk of the burned target are decreased by 5% for 2s.
