Upgrade 2.Description:
Charge forward on a wave, shoving enemies for 0.5s and leaving them unable to act for 0.5s. Mew gains a shield for 3s and becomes immune to hindrances while using this move. At the end of this move, Mew can jump in a designated direction and becomes immune to hindrances for a short time. If this move hits an enemy, Mew gains an additional boost counter.

Through Light Screen: Distance the wave travels is increased and Light Screen attaches to Mew.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the shield effect of this move.

Move 2.Name: Coaching
Move 2.Description: Teleport to an ally, granting them a shield for 3s and increasing basic attack speed by 40% for 2s. If this move is used on an ally who hasn't been coached within 7s, the cooldown of this move is reduced by 90%. If this move is used on an ally who has been recently coached, this move goes on cooldown. The cooldown of Electro Ball, Solar Beam, and Surf is reduced by 1s each time this move is used.

Upgrade 1.Name: Light Screen
Upgrade 1.Description: Create a translucent wall in the designated location for 4s that prevents enemies from passing and shoves them for 0.5s if they make contact with the wall. If Mew's Electro Ball, Solar Beam, or Surf passes through the wall, the effects of those moves will be enhanced (such as increased damage, range, and area-of-effect). If this move is used again within 4s, the wall is pulled towards Mew and follows them as they move for an additional 4s. While the wall moves with Mew, enemy attacks that pass through it will deal 25% reduced damage but will not prevent enemies from passing and does not shove them anymore.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the size of the wall.

Upgrade 2.Name: Agility
Upgrade 2.Description: Move quickly in a designated direction, increasing Mew's movement speed by 5% for 2s. Holds 2 uses in reserve and cannot be used more than once every 2s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the distance Mew moves in the designated direction.

Unite Move.Name: Mystical Mirage
Unite Move.Description: Mew becomes unstoppable and floats in the air for 1s, then becoming invincible and invisible for up to 6s until using an attack or move. A field is created around Mew for 5s This field travels with Mew. Mew and all allies within the area become stealthed for 4s. After the field expires, this move deals damage to enemies within the area-of-effect.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Battle Armor
Passive Ability.Description:
The critical-hit modifier of enemies is reduced by 50% when dealing damage to Falinks. Falinks can hold 10 more Aeos Energy than other Pokémon.

Falinks is a group comprised of the Brass and five Troopers. Each member of the group has a separate hitbox and counts as a different target. The Brass always takes 10% reduced damage. Troopers will take 90% less damage while attached to the Brass. For attacks or moves that can hit multiple targets, the total damage Falinks can take from a single move or attack is 110% of that move or attack's damage.

Falinks.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, or after the user uses Tackle, Megahorn, or Iron Head. Boosted attacks also apply a 50% slow for 1s to opposing Pokémon they hit.

When a boosted attack hits, the cooldown of Tackle, Megahorn, or Iron Head is reduced by 2.5s

Move 1.Name: Tackle
Move 1.Description: Charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and shoving them. The user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.

Upgrade 1.Name: Megahorn
Upgrade 1.Description:
Charge with its horns, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and throwing them in the air for 0.25s. Immediately afterward, strike with its horns again and gain a shield for 2.5s.

In No Retreat formation: Falinks becomes hindrance resistant and charges power for 1s. It then strikes forward with its horns, dealing additional damage to opposing Pokémon it hits based on their max HP and gaining a shield for 2.5s. This version of the move has a 4s cd that is separate from the column version.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the shield value.

Upgrade 2.Name: Iron Head
Upgrade 2.Description:
Attacks the designated area with its steel-hard heads, dealing damage and applying 50% slow for 1.5s to opposing Pokémon it hits, as well as granting itself a shield.

In No Retreat formation: The move's range is shortened but the area of effect is widened. Pokémon hit in the landing area are also knocked up and briefly stunned for 0.33s. After using this move, for the next 4 attacks, the user can attack while moving with the Brass and Troops all deal damage separately in front of them.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Iron Head and the Troopers returning deal an additional 2% max hp damage to enemies they hit, capped to 400.

Move 2.Name: Bulk Up
Move 2.Description: Increases the user's Defense, Special Defense and Attack Speed by 25%, and Attack by 30% for 3s

Upgrade 1.Name: No Retreat
Upgrade 1.Description:
Switch to No Retreat formation while shoving enemies for 0.4s in front of Falinks and become unstoppable during the formation change for 0.5s. While in No Retreat formation, the user cannot change the direction they're facing, unless they use the other move. Falinks' movement speed when moving backward is slowed by 10%, but Attack is increased by 25% and damage taken from the front is reduced by 20%. If this move is used while the user is in No Retreat formation, the user will switch to Column formation.

While in No Retreat formation both the Brass and Troopers take 20% less damage from the front. The Brass' total damage reduction becomes 28%; the Trooper's total damage reduction becomes 92%.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Attack increase is increased to 40% and damage reduction from the front is increased to 30%.

Upgrade 2.Name: Beat Up
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. The user then switches to Dispatch formation, sending out two Troopers toward enemies that were hit. The dispatched Troopers will continue to attack enemies, but they will return to the Brass after they either: take a set amount of damage, become separated from the Brass by more than a set distance, or after 5s. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve for this move with a 0.5s cooldown between each use.

While in Dispatch formation, the Troopers sent out no longer have any damage resistance nor share damage taken with the Brass. Dispatched Troopers share the same stats as the Brass, except for HP.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Also restores HP when this move hits.

Unite Move.Name: Dust Devil Formation
Unite Move.Description:
Has the five troopers run continuously around the Brass at great speed for 5s, dealing damage and slowing enemies hit by 20% for 1s. Falinks is unstoppable during the formation change for 0.25s. While this move is being used, damage taken by the troopers is reduced. While in Dust Devil formation the Troopers take 30% less damage making the Trooper's total damage reduction 93%.

Moving counter to the rotation of the troopers will grant more hits against that target, and moving along the rotation will reduce the number of hits.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Thick Fat
Passive Ability.Description: When Mamoswine deals damage, Defense and Sp. Def are increased for 3s, stacking up to 3 times. When Mamoswine uses Ice Shard, Icicle Crash or Ice Fang, the next basic attack is boosted. If any of these moves creates a freezing area of effect inside an ally goal zone, the goal-scoring speed of enemies is decreased.

Mamoswine.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing damage and freezing enemies for a short time when it hits. Mamoswine's basic attacks deal increased damage for a short time to enemies recently frozen with one of Mamoswine's moves.

Move 1.Name: Ice Shard
Move 1.Description: Hurls 3 chunks of ice at the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. If an enemy is hit by all three chunks, they're left frozen for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Icicle Crash
Upgrade 1.Description:
Drops icicles on the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. The final icicle shatters after a set amount of time or when hit by Mamoswine's other moves or boosted attacks, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them frozen. If the icicle is shattered by Tackle or High Horsepower, the ground is covered in ice, which increases the movement speed of allies that run over it. The next basic attack is boosted.

The final icicle leaves enemies hit unable to act for a short time.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: The final icicle leaves enemies hit unable to act for .2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Ice Fang
Upgrade 2.Description:
Uses cold-infused fangs to throw the opposing Pokémon closest to the user, dealing damage and leaving them frozen for a short time. The user can then designate a direction to slam the thrown Pokémon forward or backward, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them frozen for a short time. The next basic attack is boosted.

Increases the area of effect in which enemies are left frozen after the slam attack.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the area of effect in which enemies are left frozen after the slam attack.

Move 2.Name: Tackle
Move 2.Description: Mamoswine rushes recklessly in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies they hit and throws them in the air.

Upgrade 1.Name: High Horsepower
Upgrade 1.Description:
Mamoswine rushes recklessly in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies they hit and shove them. Mamoswine then stomps their feet, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaves them unable to act for a short time. This stomping deals increased damage to frozen enemies.

Increases the reckless rush's speed and the length of time enemies are left unable to act.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the reckless rush's speed and the length of time enemies are left unable to act by .2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Earthquake
Upgrade 2.Description:
Mamoswine leaps up in the designated direction and lands forcefully, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and pulling them toward Mamoswine.

Decreases the movement speed of enemies for a short time when this move hits and increases the move's pulling power.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Decreases movement speed of enemies by 30% for 2.5s when this move hits and increases the move's pulling power.

Unite Move.Name: Mammoth Mash
Unite Move.Description: Mamoswine jumps to the designated location and becomes immune to hindrances - then repeatedly stomps the ground, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for a short time with each stomp. The final stomp throws enemies in the air.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 40% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Natural Cure
Passive Ability.Description: When Trevenant deals or receives damage while at or below 35% max HP, a continuous HP recovery effect is triggered every 0.5s for 4s. This effect also reduces the cooldowns of Trevenant's moves by 1s every 1s. When this HP recovery effect ends, there is a 100s cooldown. However, this cooldown is reduced by 1s each time one of Trevenant's moves damage an enemy. This cooldown reduction cannot be triggered more than once every 0.5s.

Trevenant.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage to enemies and heal for 5% max HP when this boosted attack hits.

Move 1.Name: Will-o-Wisp
Move 1.Description: Shoot 3 sinister flames in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and leaving them burned for 3s. This burn reduces enemy Attack by 10% and Sp. Attack by 5% for 3s. The first wisp to hit a target deals full damage, and subsequent flame hits deal 50% damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Wood Hammer
Upgrade 1.Description: Trevenant pulls back its arms and attacks, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and pulls them in front of Trevenant, leaving them unable to act for 0.5s. If this attack deals damage to at least one enemy, a follow-up attack will be available for 5s. This follow-up attack slams down a giant wooden hammer in front of Trevenant, dealing damage to any enemy it hits, and leaving them unable to act. The lower Trevenant's HP, the longer the enemy will be left unable to act. Every time this move is used, it consumes 5% of Trevenant's current HP.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: If an enemy is hit by the second hammer, the damage they deal is reduced by 20% for 3s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Curse
Upgrade 2.Description: Trevenant creates a cursed zone in exchange for 2% max HP per second for 6s, dealing damage over time to enemies in the area of effect every 0.5s for 6s and decreasing their movement speed for 1s, refreshing with each tick. The lower Trevenant's HP is, the greater the decrease in movement speed. While using this move, Trevenant builds up Curse gauge every time it deals or receives damage. Curse will deal additional damage to enemies in the area of effect when the Curse releases at the end of 6s or by manually detonating the Curse. If the Curse gauge is at maximum when it’s released, the cooldowns of Branch Poke, Horn Leech, and Pain Split are reset and opponents hit are unable to act for 0.6s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increased radius.

Move 2.Name: Branch Poke
Move 2.Description: Trevenant jabs in the designated direction; dealing damage, decreasing their movement speed by 40% for 2s, and healing Trevenant for 9% max HP (over 3 ticks as 1 tick per second) when hitting an enemy.

Upgrade 1.Name: Horn Leech
Upgrade 1.Description: Charge forward, becoming unstoppable, dealing damage to enemies and shoving them while dashing in the designated direction for the duration of the dash. At the end of the charge, enemies that were shoved are dealt additional damage and then thrown for 0.75s. Colliding with an enemy while charging recovers Trevenant's HP 3 times, and the lower Trevenant's remaining HP the more HP that is recovered. After this move hits an enemy, Trevenant's next three auto attacks deal more damage and reduce this move’s cooldown by 0.5s when they hit.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: After using this move, Trevenant's movement speed is increased by 30% for 1.5s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Pain Split
Upgrade 2.Description: Creates a link between Trevenant and a designated enemy for 6s. While the link holds, a percentage of damage that Trevenant would take is mitigated and instead redirected to the linked enemy as True damage. The lower Trevenant's remaining HP, the greater the percentage of damage that is redirected. If Trevenant's percentage of remaining HP is lower than the linked enemy, this move continuously deals damage and recovers Trevenant's HP every 0.5s. If Trevenant and the linked enemy are too far apart, the link will disappear. Using this move increases Trevenant's movement speed by 35% for 2s decaying by 7.5% per half second.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Duration increased to 8.5s.

Unite Move.Name: Phantom Forest
Unite Move.Description: Briefly becomes unstoppable and creates a cursed forest, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and marking them, throwing them, and decreasing their movement speed by 50% for 2s. Trevenant and allies deal increased basic (and boosted) attack damage to enemies that were marked by this move (0.5s cd for each attacker). The mark lasts for 6s. Trevenant is also granted a shield for 6s when this move hits an enemy with the shield being strengthened for each enemy hit, up to 5 times. While Trevenant is shielded by this shield, HP recovery effects applied to Trevenant are increased by 50%.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Magician
Passive Ability.Description: In battles where the map is divided into an ally team side and an opposing team side, this ability creates a hole under one of the berries on the opposing team's side (chosen at random) and warps it in front of Hoopa. If there are no berries, but there is Aeos Energy on the ground, then some energy is warped in front of Hoopa instead. In the event that no berries from the opposing team's side are warped, the cooldown of this ability is reduced.

Hoopa.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing damage to enemies by unleashing a beam of light. When Hoopa is unbound, every attack becomes a boosted attack and deals damage to up to 2 additional nearby enemies.

Move 1.Name: Astonish
Move 1.Description: Deals damage to enemies in the designated area of effect, leaving them unable to act for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Phantom Force
Upgrade 1.Description:
Hoopa creates a portal ring under itself and warps to a designated location, dealing damage to nearby enemies at that location and shoving them. Hoopa then shoots two sinister flames that deal damage to enemies hit and decreases their movement speed for a short time. This move can be used a second time within a set amount of time. If used a second time, Hoopa can return to the initial location they warped from. When using this move, if there is a nearby ally that is linked by Trick, that ally can also warp to the initial ring's location. The ally that warps in this way will recover HP.

Become invincible for a short time when this move is used.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Become invincible for .6s when this move is used.

Upgrade 2.Name: Shadow Ball
Upgrade 2.Description:
Unleash a shadowy blob in front of Hoopa, dealing damage to enemies hit and decreases their movement speed and Sp. Def for a short time. If Hoopa then hits the same enemy with a move or boosted attack within a set amount of time, it deals additional damage and leaves the enemy unable to act for a short time. When using this move, if there is a nearby ally linked by Trick, this move will also unleash a shadowy blob at the same target from that ally's location.

The shadowy blob will now explode before disappearing. Enemies hit by the explosion are affected as if they were hit by the shadowy blob itself.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: The shadowy blob will now explode before disappearing. Enemies hit by the explosion are affected as if they were hit by the shadowy blob itself.

Move 2.Name: Confusion
Move 2.Description: Unleash a telekinetic force in the designated direction, dealing damage to the first enemy hit. The telekinetic force clings to that enemy and then causes a delayed explosion. The explosion deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and decreases their movement speed for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Hyperspace Hole
Upgrade 1.Description:
Hoopa creates a large ring at the designated location, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect. After a set amount of time, Hoopa and any allies in the area of effect will return to their base, and their movement speed will be increased for a short time. These allies can also warp back to where the ring was created. An intact ally forward goal zone can also be designated as the location where the ring is created. Creating the ring on this forward goal zone of the top path will also create a ring on the forward goal zone of the bottom path, and vice versa. All allies, including Hoopa, can use these rings to warp between the two goal zones, and their movement speed will be increased for a short time after warping. After an ally warps, there is a set cooldown before that ally can use these rings again. If a path's forward goal zone is destroyed, the middle goal zone on the same path will take its place. This move cannot be used on base goal zones.

Also increases the movement speed of allies near the ring when they are heading towards it.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Also increases movement speed by 70% for allies near the ring when they are heading towards it.

Upgrade 2.Name: Trick
Upgrade 2.Description:
Hoopa creates a link between itself and an ally using a ring, increasing both of their movement speeds and granting them each a shield for a short time. When Hoopa uses Shadow Ball or performs a basic attack, the ring on the linked ally also unleashes the same move or attack. Hoopa can also use this move on themself, increasing movement speed and granting a shield for a short time. Additionally, it increases the damage dealt by Shadow Ball.

Also restores the linked ally Pokémon's HP when it deals damage to enemies.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Hoopa and the linked ally each heal for 10% of their own damage dealt. This effect lasts for 3s.

Unite Move.Name: Rings Unbound
Unite Move.Description:
Transforms Hoopa into Hoopa Unbound for a short time, increasing max HP and creating a ring at the designated location. Each ally can warp to the ring's location one time. As Hoopa Unbound, moves change to Hyperspace Fury and Psybeam. Hoopa Unbound cannot score goals or activate the Magician ability.

The portal ring created stays open for 10s, and allies can warp to that location once within the duration of the ring. This portal is indicated on the mini map where all players may see where it is.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Guts
Passive Ability.Description: When Larvitar receives damage equal to at least 10% of max HP, Attack is increased by 30% for 3s. This Ability goes on cooldown after it’s triggered (40s cd).
Passive Ability.Description 2: When Pupitar receives damage equal to at least 10% max HP, all status conditions are nullified and Pupitar is granted a shield worth 10% Max HP. This Ability goes on cooldown after it’s triggered (40s cd).

Tyranitar.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits.

After evolving to Pupitar basic and boosted attacks (and basic attacks during Tyrannical Rampage) have an additional 5% lifesteal that is also active when doing true damage.

Move 1.Name: Bite
Move 1.Description: Deals damage to enemies in front of the user. If the enemy HP is below 50%—or falls below that percentage as a result of this move—they become immobilized for 0.6s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dark Pulse
Upgrade 1.Description:
Exhale a mist of darkness in a horizontal sweeping motion, dealing damage to any enemy hit. If the remaining HP of an enemy hit by this move falls below 50%, they become immobilized for 0.6s. If this move hits the same enemy again within 8s, it deals additional damage.

If this move deals damage while Tyranitar's piercing strength is activated by Ancient Power, the 8s duration of piercing damage is refreshed.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Stone Edge
Upgrade 2.Description: Deals damage to enemies in a 90 degree cone, dealing more damage the closer the enemies are to the user. This move can be used up to 3 times with the cone widening to 135 and 180 degrees.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increased damage.

Move 2.Name: Rock Polish
Move 2.Description: Charge ahead in the designated direction, dealing damage to any enemies hit. If this move hits any enemy, all damage is increased by 15% for 3s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Ancient Power
Upgrade 1.Description:
Charge power before releasing a shock wave. The shock wave deals damage to nearby enemies and leaves them stunned for 1s. While charging power: Tyranitar becomes resistant to hindrances for 4s, movement speed is increased by 20% for 3s (stacking 3 times), and Tyranitar gains a shield (stacking 3 times and lasting up to 5s). If any enemies are hit by the shock wave, Tyranitar is granted piercing strength allowing damage dealt to ignore enemy Defense and shield effects for 8s.

If this move hits an enemy, a second shock wave can be released in front of Tyranitar; dealing damage to enemies and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s. If this second shock wave hits successfully, Tyranitar is granted another shield. The strength of this shield increases for each enemy hit, capping at 3 stacks.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the effect of the shields granted by this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Sand Tomb
Upgrade 2.Description:
Leap in a designated direction; dealing damage to any enemies hit and leaving them unable to act for 1.5s. When Tyranitar lands, the shock from landing creates a cloud of dust at the point of impact for 6s; dealing damage-over-time every 0.5s and decreasing movement speed of enemies hit by 35% for 1s every second.

If this move is used again while Tyranitar is in the cloud of dust, enemies in front of Tyranitar will be pulled closer to it. Furthermore, while the user is in the cloud of dust, they take 15% reduced damage and can ignore the Defense and shield effects of enemies.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the length of time the cloud of dust remains at the point of impact to 8s.

Unite Move.Name: Tyrannical Rampage
Unite Move.Description:
Briefly becomes unstoppable and explodes with anger, then goes on a rampage for 10s, gaining 50% tenacity for the duration. While rampaging, changes the auto attack pattern, dealing increased damage.

Damaging a Pokémon from the opposing team below 20% max HP with these auto attacks deals 40% max HP as true damage to the target (sufficient damage reduction effects can prevent the target from being knocked out).
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Triage
Passive Ability.Description:
Increases movement speed by 25% when near ally Pokémon at 50% max HP or below. Stores up to 8 flowers; consumes all flowers when using Synthesis, Floral Healing, or Sweet Kiss while attached, boosting their effects. Gains 1 flower every 1.5s. Gains 4 flowers when entering tall grass while detached (5s CD). Attaches to a designated ally Pokémon when using Synthesis, Floral Healing, or Sweet Kiss while detached, granting a Triage shield effect to the ally based on Comfey's max HP.

While attached: becomes untargetable, restores 1% max HP every 1s; takes Aeos energy gained in excess of the maximum the ally can hold, and scores the same amount of Aeos energy into a goal as the ally if not using a move while the ally channels the score. Detaches from the ally in the designated direction when the button to return to base is used (or a separate detach button on mobile). When detaching: removes the Triage shield effect from the ally; becomes invincible for 0.5s, and readies a boosted attack.

Comfey.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage and gaining 2 flowers when it hits.

Move 1.Name: Synthesis
Move 1.Description:
If detached attaches to the designated ally Pokémon and grants them a Triage shield effect.

If attached to an ally Pokémon: restores the HP of the ally. Restores additional HP with for each flower that is consumed (up to 8 flowers).

Upgrade 1.Name: Floral Healing
Upgrade 1.Description:
If detached: attaches to the designated ally Pokémon and grants them a Triage shield effect.

If attached to an ally Pokémon: restores the HP of the ally; restores additional HP for each flower that is consumed (up to 8 flowers). Converts any HP restored in excess of the ally Pokémon’s max HP into an overheal shield effect for 5s when this move is used (up to 4 times, refreshing duration with additional uses); adds additional value to the overheal shield effect for each additional flower that is consumed.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: While attached: increases the movement speed of the ally Pokémon by 30% for 2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Sweet Kiss
Upgrade 2.Description:
If detached: attaches to the designated ally Pokémon and grants them a Triage shield effect; adds additional shield value to the Triage shield effect with Sweet Kiss.

If attached to an ally Pokémon: charges power for up to 2.5s, reaching maximum charge after 1.5s; then infatuates opposing Pokémon in the area of effect when released for a minimum of 0.6s. Increases the range of this move with charging. Infatuated Pokémon are unable to act and approach the user against their will. Infatuates opposing Pokémon hit for an additional 0.1s for each flower that is consumed (up to an additional 0.8s with 8 flowers; maximum 1.4s). (Note: it is impossible to use Sweet Kiss with fewer than 2 flowers due to the rate of gaining flowers and the cooldown reduction cap.)
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: While attached: increases the movement speed of the ally Pokémon by 50% for 2s while charging power.

Move 2.Name: Vine Whip
Move 2.Description:
Strikes out with whiplike vines in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the line area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 40% for 2s when it hits.

While attached to an ally: increases this move's range.

Upgrade 1.Name: Magical Leaf
Upgrade 1.Description:
Launches 3 curious, scattering leaves every 1s for 5s (up to 18 leaves) at random opposing Pokémon in the area of effect (even when in tall grass or in stealth), prioritizing Pokémon from the opposing team; damaging opposing Pokémon and applying a mark to them for 4s when hit by a leaf. When a marked target is damaged by 6 leaves, stuns them for 0.5s.

While attached to an ally: increases this move's range of targeting.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the number of leaves launched to 4 leaves every 1s (maximum 24 leaves).

Upgrade 2.Name: Grass Knot
Upgrade 2.Description:
Unleashes vines in the designated direction, damaging up to two opposing Pokémon hit and immobilizing them. If this move hits two opposing Pokémon (within 75% of this move's max range from each other), pull the second target to the first target's location and deal additional damage to both.

While attached to an ally: increases this move's range.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases this move’s area of effect.

Unite Move.Name: Flowery Fields Forever
Unite Move.Description:
Briefly becomes unstoppable and creates a large area of tall grass around their location for 6s. Pokémon in this tall grass cannot be seen, just as with other tall grass on the map. Restores the HP of teammates in this Unite Move's tall grass every 0.5s.

Gains special flowers from this tall grass, which grant access to an empowered use of Floral Healing or Sweet Kiss.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Steadfast
Passive Ability.Description: While at low HP, gain a shield and increased movement speed. (30s cooldown)

Lucario.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, performing a combo attack with a bone. Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect and throws them.

Move 1.Name: Quick Attack
Move 1.Description: Has the user lunge forward at a speed that makes it almost invisible, dealing damage to enemies it comes in contact with.

Upgrade 1.Name: Extreme Speed
Upgrade 1.Description:
Charge forward with breathtaking speed. If this move hits an enemy from point-blank range, its cooldown is reset and it restores the user's HP. However, the cooldown cannot be continually reset by using this move on the same enemy multiple times in succession.

Increases Attack for a short time when this move is used.

After learning this move: nearby opposing Pokémon have an Extreme Speed marker automatically applied to them. If this move's dash hits an opposing Pokémon: resets this move's cooldown; and removes the marker from the first target hit for 10s (or until applied by other moves).
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the user's Attack by 7.5% for 2s when this move is used.

Upgrade 2.Name: Power-Up Punch
Upgrade 2.Description:
Charges power before unleashing a powerful attack. While the user is charging power, its movement speed is decreased but its Attack slowly increases and the damage it receives is reduced. The user then charges in the designated direction and releases this move, dealing more damage the lower the enemies remaining HP is. The next basic attack becomes a boosted attack if the move hits. This move's cooldown is reduced every time one of the user's moves hits.

Become immune to hindrances while charging power.

Against wild Pokémon: additional missing HP damage is capped at 1000.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Becomes unstoppable while charging this move.

Move 2.Name: Meteor Mash
Move 2.Description: Blast forward with a punch like a comet, dealing damage to enemies and shoving them.

Upgrade 1.Name: Bone Rush
Upgrade 1.Description:
Performs a combo attack with a bone, dealing multiple damaging blows to enemies and shoving them. The final attack sends the bone flying. If used again while the bone is traveling, the user rushes to the location of the bone, dealing damage to nearby enemies.

Using this move again will reset the cooldown for Extreme Speed or Power-Up Punch.

Landing one of the three initial strikes on opposing Pokémon applies an Extreme Speed marker to them.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Catching the bone with the second use resets the cooldown of Extreme Speed or Power-Up Punch.

Upgrade 2.Name: Close Combat
Upgrade 2.Description:
Unleash a combo attack, dealing multiple blows to enemies and shoving them.

Increased damage.

Changes the next basic attack used within 5s after using this move. This auto attack gains increased range and lunges at an opposing Pokémon with a kick, dealing damage.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Unite Move.Name: Aura Cannon
Unite Move.Description:
Deals damage to enemies in the area of effect. Increases the damage dealt by the user's next Power-Up Punch after using this move.

If the user learned Extreme Speed: applies an Extreme Speed marker to targets hit by this Unite Move. If the user learned Power-Up Punch: strengthens the damage of the next attack of Power-Up Punch by roughly 20% and the shove duration to 1s when Power-Up Punch is released.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Oblivious
Passive Ability.Description: When a hindrance is inflicted on Bounsweet, Steenee; decrease the duration of the effect.
Passive Ability.Description 2: When a hindrance is inflicted on Tsareena; decrease the duration of the effect. Using a boosted attack or selectable move grants 1 stack of Queenly Majesty to Tsareena. After gaining 3 stacks, the Queenly Majesty buff is applied to Tsareena for 5s, which allows one of Tsareena's moves to be reused (these uses do not share the same cooldown as the base moves), dealing increased damage and applying an additional effect based on the move used. Boosted attacks will refresh the duration of fully stacked Queenly Majesty.

Tsareena.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, lunging at an opposing Pokémon, damage nearby opposing Pokémon and lowering the Defense of those hit by 20% for 1s.

Move 1.Name: Razor Leaf
Move 1.Description: Pelts leaves in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. The critical-hit rate of this move is higher than that of basic attacks.

Upgrade 1.Name: Triple Axel
Upgrade 1.Description:
Tsareena spin-kicks 3 times in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies. The damage dealt increases each time a kick hits, and basic attack speed is increased for a short time after this move ends by an amount based on the number of landed kicks. Queenly Majesty Effect: Restores HP each time a kick hits.

Increased damage.

Queenly Majesty Effect: Restores HP per opposing Pokémon hit with each kick.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increased damage.

Upgrade 2.Name: Stomp
Upgrade 2.Description:
Tsareena jumps up high and attacks enemies their feet. When this move hits, deal damage to enemies and leave them unable to act for a short time. Queenly Majesty effect: Restores HP when this move hits.

Increased damage.

Queenly Majesty Effect: Restores HP per opposing Pokémon hit.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Move 2.Name: Rapid Spin
Move 2.Description: Tsareena spins in the designated direction, dealing damage to any enemy hit. When this move hits, increase Tsareena's movement speed for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Trop Kick
Upgrade 1.Description:
Perform a flying kick in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and decrease their Attack for a short time. Enemies that make contact with the flying kick's wake receive damage and have their movement speed decreased for a short time. Queenly Majesty effect: Grants Tsareena a shield when this move is used.

Increased damage.

Queenly Majesty Effect: Grants the user a shield for 3s when this move is used.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Grassy Glide
Upgrade 2.Description:
Tsareena slides in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and decreases their movement speed for a short time. This is followed by an attack in the opposite direction, dealing damage and shoving enemies hit. Queenly Majesty effect: Grants Tsareena a shield when this move is used.

Increased damage.

Queenly Majesty Effect: Grants the user a shield for 3s when this move is used.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Unite Move.Name: Queen Ascendant
Unite Move.Description: Tsareena leaps toward an enemy from the opposing team and kicks it high into the air before showering it with a series of kicks while airborne, dealing damage to them. The moment Tsareena lands on the ground, recover HP. While airborne, the enemy that is being kicked and Tsareena are not affected by other moves.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Torrent
Passive Ability.Description:
When this Pokémon deals damage to opposing Pokémon, or as time passes, the Torrent gauge increases to a max of 100. If a move is used when the Torrent gauge exceeds 50, 50 points of the gauge is consumed and the move becomes boosted.

The rate at which the Torrent gauge increases depends on your current HP with thresholds at: above 66% HP, below 66% HP, and below 33% HP. Every second gain 1/2/3 Torrent gauge.

Empoleon.Attack:
Piplup shoots a blast of water, becoming a boosted attack with every third attack dealing increased damage. Basic and Boosted attacks increase Torrent gauge by 2/3/4.

When evolved into Prinplup or Empoleon, basic attacks become melee ranged. The user slices with their wings, becoming a boosted attack with every third attack dealing increased damage. Boosted attack increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20. This Pokémon heals 15% of damage dealt with basic and boosted attacks.

Move 1.Name: Water Gun
Move 1.Description:
The user blasts a forceful shot of water in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move deals increased damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Hydro Cannon
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user launches a watery blast in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and stunning them for 0.5s. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move leaves behind a whirlpool for 3s which deals damage to opposing Pokémon, and heals the user when they stand in it, every 0.5s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this moves cooldown by 30% of the remaining cooldown when it hits Pokémon from the opposing team.

Upgrade 2.Name: Whirlpool
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user becomes unstoppable for 1s as they create a violent swirling whirlpool around themself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon every 0.25s up to 4 hits. The user's HP is restored by 40% of the damage dealt against Pokémon on the opposing team, and by 20% against wild Pokémon. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move will throw opposing Pokémon for 0.6s and the user will slash once at the end of the move in the designated direction extending the unstoppable duration to 1.33s. The healing of the move is increased to 50% of the damage dealt against Pokémon on the opposing team, and 25% against wild Pokémon.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increased damage.

Move 2.Name: Peck
Move 2.Description:
The user jabs its sharply pointed beak in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move applies a 40% slow for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Metal Claw
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user charge towards a designated target, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon with wings of steel and shoving them for 0.25s. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move grants the user a shield for 3s. This shield will not stack with itself.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increased the shield from Torrent.

Upgrade 2.Name: Aqua Jet
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user lunge at blinding speed in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and increasing the user's basic attack speed by 40% for 5s. Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when under 50 Torrent gauge.

When empowered by Torrent, this move will also lower the special defense of those hit by 60% for 3s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Lowers the base cooldown of this move by 1s.

Unite Move.Name: Sovereign Slide
Unite Move.Description:
Has the user slide at high speed in the designated direction and form a whirlpool on impact, or when the maximum distance is reached, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and throwing them for 1s. The whirlpool remains centered on the user for 7s and applies a 50% slow to opposing Pokémon in the area. The slow lasts 1s when opposing Pokémon leave the area. Empoleon is unstoppable throughout this move. The longer the user slides, the faster the recovery to perform other actions.

Increases Torrent gauge by 10/15/20 when the move connects. While the whirlpool is active the amount of Torrent gauge gained per second is increased to 12/18/24.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Hadron Engine
Passive Ability.Description: Create Electric Terrain around Miraidon for 10s when hitting an enemy player with a Move. If Miraidon has Aeos Energy, up to 40 energy (in multiples of 10) will be consumed when the Electric Terrain is created, increasing the size of the Electric Terrain. Miraidon and Pokémon cannot be put to sleep on Electric Terrain. Miraidon on the Electric Terrain deals 30% additional Move damage while allies will deal 10% additional Move damage. Electric Terrain also strengthens ally team goals' healing and shielding by 30% and weakens enemy team goals' healing and shielding by 30%. This ability goes on a 30s cooldown after it's triggered.

Miraidon.Attack: Miraidon does not have a boosted attack.

Move 1.Name: Thunder Shock
Move 1.Description: Fire a jolt of electricity in a line in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Charge Beam
Upgrade 1.Description: Charge power before firing an electric beam in a designated direction. If this move hits an enemy, Miraidon's Sp. Atk is increased by 10% for 6s and stores electric power, stacking twice. If electric power is stored twice within a short time, Charge Beam becomes Boosted. Boosted Charge Beam can be held for up to 10s and continuously fires an electric beam in a designated direction, dealing damage to all enemies in the area of effect and granting Miraidon Hindrance Resistance. Miraidon may move while firing the electric beam but may not change the original direction. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve. There is a 1s cooldown between uses.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces the cooldown to charge by 0.5s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Electro Drift
Upgrade 2.Description: Race forward while spinning, dealing damage to enemies in the path. If this move hits an enemy, Miraidon stores electric power. If electric power is stored twice within a short time, Electro Drift becomes Boosted. Boosted Electro Drift can be held for up to 10s and homes in on a designated enemy with the spinning attack, dealing damage to any enemy hit. This move deals increased damage instead if the remaining HP of the targets hit are below 50%. During this move, Miraidon is Hindrance Resistant. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve. There is a 1s cooldown between uses.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces the cooldown to charge by 4s.

Move 2.Name: Thunder Wave
Move 2.Description: Discharges electricity at a designated enemy, dealing damage and leaving them paralyzed, decreasing attack speed by 10% and movement speed by 30% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Thunder
Upgrade 1.Description: Summon a cloud in the skies dropping thunderbolts at set intervals. The summoned cloud moves slowly towards the nearest enemy. Thunderbolts deal damage and apply a 10% slow to enemies hit.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the slow effect to 20%.

Upgrade 2.Name: Parabolic Charge
Upgrade 2.Description: Floods the area around Miraidon with electricity, dealing damage and applying a 50% slow to enemies hit for 2s. If this move hits an enemy, Miraidon recovers HP and 1 stored use of either Charge Beam or Electro Drift. The more targets hit, the more HP Miraidon recovers.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Grants a shield when this move is used.

Unite Move.Name: Bright Future Meteor Storm
Unite Move.Description:
The user gains hindrance resistance and calls down up to 5 meteorites in a row, each at a designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon hit by each meteorite. If a meteorite hits an opposing Pokémon, it increases the damage of subsequent meteorites that hit by an additional 20% (up to 80% additional increased damage for the final meteorite if all preceding meteorites hit). The final meteorite damages opposing Pokémon in a wider area of effect and throws them for 1s when it hits.

All effects related to Unite Move use activate when the first meteorite is called down. This Unite Move ends when released, consuming 10% of the Unite Move gauge. Each meteorite called down consumes at most 30% of the Unite Move gauge.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Marvel Scale
Passive Ability.Description: Increases Defense by 100 for 1.5s when afflicted by a status condition (Dratini and Dragonair only).
Passive Ability.Description 2: When damaged by an enemy player, gain a buff that reduces damage taken by 30% for 2.5 seconds. This ability has a 15 second cooldown.

Dragonite.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, blasting water in front of Dratini, dealing increased damage, reducing the cooldown of Dratini's moves by 1s, and recover 5% max HP when this hits.

Move 1.Name: Twister
Move 1.Description: Creates a twister and releases it in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and decreases their movement speed for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dragon Dance
Upgrade 1.Description: Dash in a designated direction, in one of three cycling patterns. After using this move, grant a buff increasing attack speed, movement speed, Attack by 8% (capping at 24%), as well as increasing the stage of Hyper Beam. This buff stacks up to 3 times, and the duration is reset to full on reuse. All stacks are removed after a few seconds. Basic attacks reduce the cooldown of this ability by 1s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Further increases Dragonite's movement speed by 10% for 4.5s if there are enemies nearby when the dance ends.

Upgrade 2.Name: Extreme Speed
Upgrade 2.Description: Dash toward the designated location stopping upon contact with an enemy, dealing damage and throwing them.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: If Dragonite's movement speed is currently decreased when this move is used, that movement speed decrease is negated before the move is used.

Move 2.Name: Dragon Breath
Move 2.Description: Exhale a mighty gust in the designated direction. If this move hits an enemy, the user's next attack becomes a boosted attack.

Upgrade 1.Name: Hyper Beam
Upgrade 1.Description:
Fires a beam of energy toward the targeted enemy after a delay, dealing damage to all enemies that it hits, as well as additional damage based on their Max HP. The move changes Stages based on the number of stacks of Dragon Dance that Dragonite has, from 0 to 3. The Max HP damage is 5%/10%/15%/20%, determined by the current Stage.  Dragonite cannot act during the duration of this move.

Reduces damage taken by 50% while charging this move.

Against Wild Pokémon: The additional damage from max HP is capped at 600/1200/1800/2400 depending on the stage.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces damage taken by 50% while charging this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Outrage
Upgrade 2.Description: Dragonite begins rampaging for 5s. This move starts with a ground stomp, dealing damage to enemies in an area around Dragonite. While rampaging, all of Dragonite's basic attacks become a melee boosted attack, alternating through the water, electric, and fire boosted attack types. When the rampage ends, Dragonite is left briefly unable to act.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduce the duration of hindrances inflicted on Dragonite while rampaging by 40% for 5.5s.

Unite Move.Name: Draco Impact
Unite Move.Description: Briefly fly up in the air before crashing down with several comets on a designated area, dealing damage and throwing enemies in the area. The farther Dragonite flies, the more the Unite guage is consumed (25% at min disatance and 100% at max). Dragonite is unstoppable while using this move.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 35% Attack Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Blaze
Passive Ability.Description: When using a move while at low remaining HP, deal additional damage with a fireball to enemies. This Ability goes on cooldown after it’s triggered.

Delphox.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage when it hits.

Move 1.Name: Ember
Move 1.Description: Shoot a small flame in front of itself, dealing damage to enemies hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Fire Blast
Upgrade 1.Description:
Launch an intense, all-consuming fire in a forward direction; dealing damage to enemies hit. As the fire travels, it leaves behind a zone of flames in which a delayed explosion will occur, dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect.

Increased damage.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Damage increased.

Upgrade 2.Name: Mystical Fire
Upgrade 2.Description:
Unleash a special, hot ball of fire in a forward direction. If it hits an enemy, the fire will explode; dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect.

Also reduces cooldowns of all moves if this move hits. If it hits an enemy from the opposing team, cooldowns are reduced even further.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Also reduces cooldowns of all moves if this move hits by 1s. If it hits an enemy player, cooldowns are reduced even further (1.5s).

Move 2.Name: Will-o-Wisp
Move 2.Description: Conjure up three sinister flames and launch them toward the designated area. These flames then explode; dealing damage to enemies hit by the explosion and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Fire Spin
Upgrade 1.Description:
Create a vortex of fire in the designated area. The vortex automatically moves toward the nearest enemy, dealing damage-over-time to all enemies caught in the area for a short time and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 5s. After 4.5s passes or an enemy is caught in the center of the vortex; the flames increase in intensity. This increases the rate at which the flames deal damage and also leaves the enemies unable to act for 1s. This trapped damage ticks four times total. The Fire Spin will otherwise deal damage as it travels. Move placement on top of enemies will apply a "searching" tick and then apply "trapped" damage. A maximum of 9 ticks is possible.

Applies a debuff on trap activation that increases all damage dealt by Delphox by 15% while enemies are trapped. Debuff is only cleansable after the first tick of trap damage and does not re-apply the debuff.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Applies a debuff on trap activation that increases all damage dealt by Delphox by 15% for 3s. Debuff is only cleansable after the first tick of trap damage and does not re-apply the debuff.

Upgrade 2.Name: Flame Charge
Upgrade 2.Description:
Charge forward in the designated direction. If there are any enemies in range when the charge ends, summon three flames to attack. The flames deal damage and decrease enemy movement speed for a short time. This move can be used while also using Fire Blast or Mystical Fire.

Increases Delphox's movement speed for a short time when this move is used.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases Delphox's movement speed by 30% for 2s when this move is used.

Unite Move.Name: Fanciful Fireworks
Unite Move.Description: Deals damage-over-time to enemies in the designated area-of-effect. Enemies that are hit have decreased movement speed and weakened HP recovery effects for a short time.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Technician
Passive Ability.Description: After using a move, the next basic attack will change to deal 2 attacks instead. The second attack deals reduced damage.

Scizor.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, decreasing enemy movement speed by 30% for 2s when it hits and increasing Scizor's Defense by 40% for 6s. This Defense increase can stack additively up to 3 times and the duration refreshes when gaining another stack.

Move 1.Name: Fury Cutter
Move 1.Description: Slashes twice dealing damage with its scythes in a cone. The second slash deals more damage than the first.

Upgrade 1.Name: Bullet Punch
Upgrade 1.Description: Dash in the designated direction and strike enemies, reducing this move's cooldown by 40% of its current cooldown if the attack connects and recovering a portion of HP with each hit. If Bullet Punch is used again within 8s, the number of punches increases. The number of punches increases from 3 to a maximum of 5.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Also grants a shield for 3s if this move hits an enemy.

Move 2.Name: Quick Attack
Move 2.Description: Dashes in designated direction dealing damage to enemies hit along the way.

Upgrade 1.Name: Double Hit
Upgrade 1.Description:
Jumps in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies during the jump and applying a mark to that enemy until they leave combat. After hitting an enemy, jump again. An enhanced auto attack is prepared on landing that has increased range and charges at the enemy being attacked. This enhanced attack is available for 5s.

If a marked enemy is knocked out, all of the user's move cooldowns are reset. The mark may be cleansed.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Swords Dance
Upgrade 2.Description: Dashes a short distance, increasing Attack by 15% for 5s. While Swords Dance's attack buff is active, after basic attacks or abilities make contact 8 times, the user launches an area-of-effect attack that pierces through enemies in a line.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces the damage the user receives by 50% while dashing (0.3s).

Unite Move.Name: Red Illusion Dive
Unite Move.Description: Target an enemy and punch them, dealing an initial hit of damage, decreasing their movement speed by 40% for 3s. Then create 5 copies that dash away from Scizor. If a copy hits a non targetted enemy while dashing out, it deals damage, decreases that enemy's movement speed by 40% for 3s, then disappears. If untouched, the illusory copy will remain on the battlefield for 8 seconds. Enemies that come in contact with an illusary copy will detonate the copy, having damage dealt to them an slowing them for 40% for 3s. For 8s after activation, this move may be used again, dashing in a designated direction dealing damage to enemies hit. The enemies hit are also grabbed and carried while Scizor continues dashing. At the end of the dash, Scizor punches the ground dealing damage in an area-of-effect. If the grabbed enemy hits an illusory copy during the shove, they will be dealt damage and be left incapacitated for 2s.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Oblivious
Passive Ability.Description: When Slowbro receives damage, the HP loss is gradual. When damaging an opponent with a move, restore some of the remaining gradual HP loss and decrease the target's Sp. Defense.

Slowbro.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack. When this boosted attack hits, deal damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and decrease their movement speed.

Move 1.Name: Water Gun
Move 1.Description: Slowbro channels a fierce stream of water which deals damage and lowers movement speed. Increase the area of effect and damage the longer this move is channeled.

Upgrade 1.Name: Scald
Upgrade 1.Description:
Deal damage to opposing Pokémon and create steam when it hits. Opposing Pokémon inside the steam's area are left burned and have their movement speed decreased.

Increased area of effect.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the size of the steam cloud.

Upgrade 2.Name: Surf
Upgrade 2.Description:
Charge forward on a wave, dealing damage in an area and knock opponents back. This is followed by two more waves that deal damage and decrease enemy movement speed.

The second and third waves become larger and also knock enemies back like the first wave.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: The second and third waves will now also throw enemies in the air for .75s, and the second wave will knock them back.

Move 2.Name: Slack Off
Move 2.Description: Lay down and take a rest, continuously regaining health. Damage taken will reduce the recovery effect.

Upgrade 1.Name: Amnesia
Upgrade 1.Description:
Nullify some damage before it takes effect. For a short time after using this move, become immune to hindrances and gain increased defense. Also increase the damage dealt by the next use of Scald or Surf.

Increases Sp. Attack.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Also increases Slowbro's Sp. Defense by 125.

Upgrade 2.Name: Telekinesis
Upgrade 2.Description:
Make opposing Pokémon float with psychic power. If used again, pull the opposing Pokémon toward the user.

Increased range and firing speed.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the range and speed of the beam.

Unite Move.Name: Slowbeam
Unite Move.Description: Stare at an enemy and attack continually, dealing damage and rooting them. Grants a shield and makes the user immune to hindrances while using this move.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 40% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Cotton Down
Passive Ability.Description: Damages an enemy and decreases its movement speed for a short time after receiving a certain amount of damage. When that happens, also recover HP and gain increased movement speed for a short time.

Eldegoss.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, attaching a bit of cotton fluff to the enemy. The user's basic attacks against an enemy with cotton fluff on it deal increased damage and decreases the enemies movement speed for a short time.

Each teammate uses their own Sp. Atk to deal additional damage on targets with cotton fluff. This additional damage has an internal cooldown of 0.35s for each teammate.

Move 1.Name: Leafage
Move 1.Description: Pelt the target with leaves, decreasing the movement speed of enemies for a short time when it hits.

Upgrade 1.Name: Pollen Puff
Upgrade 1.Description:
Throws a Pollen Puff, which attaches to an enemy. The Pollen Puff restores HP for allies and deals damage to enemies.

If a Pollen Puff attaches to enemies, it also deals them damage over time. If a Pollen Puff attaches to an ally, it also reduces the damage they receive.

The Pollen Puff explodes on its own after 1s with the same effects if it doesn't attach to any Pokémon within the first 0.5s of landing.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: If the Pollen Puff attaches to a teammate, it reduces the damage that teammate receives by 15% for 2s before it explodes. If the Pollen Puff attaches to any Pokémon, opposing Pokémon that come in contact with the target receive damage over time every 0.5s before the Pollen Puff explodes.

Upgrade 2.Name: Leaf Tornado
Upgrade 2.Description:
Attack with a tornado of sharp leaves, leaving a path in its wake that increases ally movement speed for a short time.

Also decreases the accuracy of enemies for a short time when this move hits.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Applies a debuff to opposing Pokémon hit by the tornado that causes auto attacks to deal no damage for 2.5s.

Move 2.Name: Synthesis
Move 2.Description: Restores the HP of the user and nearby allies.

Upgrade 1.Name: Cotton Guard
Upgrade 1.Description:
Protects the user and nearby allies with cotton. The cotton absorbs damage and when the move ends, the cotton restores some HP.

Also increases the user's movement speed while using this move.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the movement speed of teammates in the area of effect by 30% for 2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Cotton Spore
Upgrade 2.Description:
Gathers spores around the user, increasing the user's movement speed while this move is in effect. The spores burst after a short time, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect, throwing them, and decreasing their movement speed for a short time.

Reduced damage taken while using this move.

Cotton Cloud Crash can be used during this move, ending the move early and making the spores burst immediately.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the user's Defense and Sp. Def by 50% for 2s.

Unite Move.Name: Cotton Cloud Crash
Unite Move.Description: Float into the air and become invincible. If used again, pound the ground, attacking and shoving enemies and restoring HP to allies in the area of effect.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Gluttony
Passive Ability.Description:
Increases the effects gained from eating Berries.

Doubles the HP restored from eating Sitrus Berries. Restores an additional 1% max HP + 0 x (Lv. - 1) + 23 HP from eating Oran Berries (from Greedent Cheek Pouch). Alcremie's Recover heals 10% more HP than usual.

Snorlax.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack. When this boosted attack hits, it deals damage to nearby enemies and decreases their movement speed for a short time.

Move 1.Name: Tackle
Move 1.Description: Charge forward belly first, dealing damage when it hits and shoving the first enemy it comes in contact with. After this move, the user's next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.

Upgrade 1.Name: Heavy Slam
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user slam its heavy body down, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and throwing them.

Increased damage.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Flail
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user flail about, increasing the user's basic attack damage the lower the user's HP is.

Increased damage. If the user has low HP while using this move, its basic attacks that hit enemies will restore the user's HP.

While flailing about, auto attacks deal increased damage, with damage strengthening for every 20% max HP the user is missing. Basic attacks used while flailing about also decrease the movement speed of opposing Pokémon hit by 20% for 1s; boosted attacks decrease movement speed by 50% for 1s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage dealt by auto attacks used while flailing about.

Move 2.Name: Rest
Move 2.Description:
Has the user fall asleep on the spot and restores its HP. Blocks the movement of all enemies while this move is in effect.

This move cannot be used within an opposing Miraidon's Electric Terrain.

Upgrade 1.Name: Block
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user spread its arms wide to create a wall and grants it a shield. Prevents enemies from passing through the wall and shoves them if they run into it.

Reduces the damage the user receives while this move is in effect.

The user can use this move again to drop the wall early, becoming vulnerable to hindrances and returning to normal movement speed. This move's cooldown begins when the wall ends.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the user's Defense and Sp. Def by 35% while holding up the wall. Shoves opposing Pokémon contacting the wall from the front not more than once every 0.5s (maximum 8 times).

Upgrade 2.Name: Yawn
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user let loose a huge yawn that lulls enemies into a sleepy haze, putting enemies hit by this move to sleep.

Decrease the movement speed of enemies for a short time when this move hits.

Targets that are hit after their deep sleep is halfway through wake up immediately.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Decreases the movement speed of opposing Pokémon waking up from a full deep sleep by 35% for 3s.

Unite Move.Name: Power Nap
Unite Move.Description:
Go to sleep and begin snoring, dealing damage over time to enemies in the area of effect and throw them. While sleeping, become immune to hindrances and continually restore HP.

After the user flips over or snores a second time, the user can use this Unite Move again to stop sleeping early, becoming vulnerable to hindrances and returning to normal.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 40% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Disguise
Passive Ability.Description: Mimikyu starts the game in Disguised Form. While in Disguised Form, when Mimikyu would take damage from an enemy, Mimikyu instead takes 5% remaining HP as piercing damage, becomes Impervious for 0.5s (immune to damage but not hindrances) and changes to Busted Form. A revenge mark is applied on the enemy that broke the disguise. Mimikyu's movement speed increases by 10% when approaching revenge-marked enemies and deals 10% increased damage to them. After 50s in Busted Form, Mimikyu returns to Disguised Form. The time it takes to return to Disguised Form is reduced by 25s when Mimikyu knocks out an enemy player and is fully reset upon respawning.

Mimikyu.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack or when using a move, striking twice with an increased area of attack.

Move 1.Name: Astonish
Move 1.Description: Shout in a startling fashion, dealing damage to enemies hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Play Rough
Upgrade 1.Description: Mimikyu dashes in a designated direction. If Mimikyu connects with an enemy, Mimikyu latches on, becoming invincible and follows the target, slowing them by 30% for 1s and dealing damage to them 5 times. Mimikyu will then leap off of the enemy in a designated direction. Hits after the first deal 70% reduced damage.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases Mimikyu's movement speed by 20% for 3s after the attack is finished.

Upgrade 2.Name: Shadow Claw
Upgrade 2.Description: Mimikyu slashes in front of themself with sharp claws, dealing damage to enemies hit. Each hit decreases enemy movement speed by 20% for 1.5s, stacking up to 3 times, and restores the user's HP. Whenever Mimikyu hits at least one enemy with a basic or boosted attack, this move's cooldown is reduced by 1s, and the number of hits when this move is used again increases. Up to 4 of these additional hits can be stored and are lost after 5s without auto attacking or after using this move.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: At the end of the move, deal an additional hit of damage equal to 12% of missing HP. This damage is capped at 500 against wild Pokémon.

Move 2.Name: Scratch
Move 2.Description:
Mimikyu charges in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s. For 5s after using this move, Scratch can be used a second time, making Mimikyu attack in front of themself, dealing damage to enemies hit and additional damage based on their missing HP.

Additional damage is equal to 10% of the enemy's missing HP. Against Wild Pokemon: The total damage is capped at 500.

Upgrade 1.Name: Shadow Sneak
Upgrade 1.Description:
Mimikyu moves backwards a short distance and extends their shadow, marking an enemy hit as a shadow target for 3s. If the shadow passes through tall grass or a wall, the shadow extends even further. If Mimikyu deals damage to an enemy who is marked as a shadow target, each hit dealt will deal additional damage equal to 4% of the target's missing HP. If this move is used again, Mimikyu moves to the enemy marked as a shadow target, fears them for 0.25s, deals damage to enemies in an area, and refresh the duration of that enemy's shadow mark. If the marked target gets knocked out Shadow Sneak's cooldown is reset and Mimikyu recovers HP.

Against Wild Pokemon: The % missing HP damage is capped at 150.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Trick Room
Upgrade 2.Description: Leap to a location and create a bizarre area around Mimikyu in a square for 6s. If a slow would be applied to Mimikyu within the area, Mimikyu instead receives a movement speed increase equal to the slow rate. While inside the Trick Room, Mimikyu receives 50% reduced damage from enemies outside of the Trick Room. This move can be used as often as desired while inside the Trick Room, teleporting Mimikyu a set distance with each use.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Unite Move.Name: Play With Me...
Unite Move.Description: Mimikyu leaps at an enemy and traps them inside Mimikyu's rag, dealing damage over time and granting the user a shield. The trapped enemy is only affected by this Unite Move and effects applied by Mimikyu before using this Unite Move. If Mimikyu loses their shield, or if 2.8s passes, the user stops attacking and jumps in the designated direction. The maximum number of hits is 7, hitting every 0.4s. The Unite buffs occur after the move ends. This ability counters Unstoppable.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Mold Breaker
Passive Ability.Description:
When damaging an opposing Pokémon with an active shield effect, 30% of the damage dealt to the shield is dealt as additional damage to the target's HP.

This Pokémon's hammer strengthens every time they hit Pokémon from the opposing team with boosted attacks or moves, increasing this Pokémon's Attack by 0.5% per target hit (up to 60 times; maximum 30% Attack). After the hammer is strengthened 20, 40, and 60 times; Rock Smash, Gigaton Hammer, and Smack Down gain increased range.

Tinkaton.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack after 12s, swinging the user's hammer in a circle around them, dealing increased damage and restoring 24% of the user's missing HP when it hits. This boosted attack remains readied until used. The time until the next boosted attack is readied is reduced faster when hit by opposing Pokémon's auto attacks.

The hammer's size and basic attack range increase upon evolving into Tinkatuff and into Tinkaton.

Move 1.Name: Rock Smash
Move 1.Description:
The user swings down their hammer with rock-crushing force, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and stunning them for 0.15s when hit.

The stronger the hammer, the larger this move's area of effect.

Upgrade 1.Name: Gigaton Hammer
Upgrade 1.Description:
The user becomes unstoppable and decreases their movement speed by 50% as they charge power for up to 1.95s, becoming fully charged after 1s, before swinging down their hammer in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and throwing them when hit. This move deals more damage and the throw duration is strengthened the longer it is charged.

The stronger the hammer, the larger this move's area of effect.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Smack Down
Upgrade 2.Description:
The user decreases their movement speed by 30% as they charge power for up to 2.4s, becoming fully charged after 0.8s, then uses their hammer to hit nearby enemies in front of the user and send a rock flying to the designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon at the rock's point of impact and decreasing the movement speed of those hit by 50% for 3s (decaying by 10% every 0.5s). The longer this move is charged, the farther the rock can fly. This move deals increased damage at maximum charge. If this move is canceled, it goes on a 2s cooldown.

The stronger the hammer, the larger this move's maximum range.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Move 2.Name: Fairy Wind
Move 2.Description: The user stirs up a fairy wind in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 1s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Ice Hammer
Upgrade 1.Description: The user leaps in the designated direction and swings down their freezing hammer at the designated location, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect, decreasing the movement speed of those hit for 2.5s by 70% decaying by 10% every 0.5s to a minimum of 40%, as well as their Attack by 30%, and their Sp. Atk by 15% for 2.5s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the Attack decrease of opposing Pokémon hit to 50% and the Sp. Atk decrease to 30%.

Upgrade 2.Name: Thief
Upgrade 2.Description:
Increases the user's movement speed by 20% for 3s, strengthened to 40% for 5s when moving toward an opposing Pokémon affected by hindrances; and empowers the user's next basic attack for 5s, swinging their hammer in front of them and dealing boosted attack damage to opposing Pokémon hit. If this Thief empowered basic attack hits a Pokémon from the opposing team, the user gains a Thief buff for 5s, increasing their Defense and Sp. Def by 10% per target hit (up to 5 times; maximum 50%). While the user has the Thief buff, the movement speed increases remain in effect; the user readies boosted attacks after 2s, and the time until the next boosted attack is reduced significantly when hit by opposing Pokémon's auto attacks.

Pokémon from the opposing team hit with Thief empowered basic attacks have a separate Thief debuff applied to them for 5s, decreasing their Defense and Sp. Def by 10%. The duration of the Thief debuff on targets hit refreshes when hit by subsequent Thief empowered attacks, and the duration of the Thief buff on the user is refreshed when a Pokémon from the opposing team is hit with the empowered attack.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the Defense and Sp. Def decrease on targets hit by the empowered auto attack to 25%; strengthens the Defense and Sp. Def increases on the user to 25% per target hit (maximum 125%).

Unite Move.Name: Kiss Bliss Kaboom
Unite Move.Description:
The user first blows cute kisses at the designated Pokémon from the opposing team and their nearby allies, damaging and stunning them for 0.7s when Kiss Bliss hits a target. The user can then use the Unite Move again, leaping up forward and slamming their hammer down at the designated location, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect of Kaboom.

Unite Move buffs apply when Kiss Bliss hits a target.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Gale Wings
Passive Ability.Description: While at high HP, movement speed is increased.

Talonflame.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack every third attack, creating a fiery whirlwind that deals damage to enemies in the area of effect. This boosted attack remains readied even after leaving combat for a prolonged period of time.

Basic and boosted attacks lunge at the target opposing Pokémon.

Move 1.Name: Peck
Move 1.Description: Fly toward an enemy and attack 3 times.

Upgrade 1.Name: Flame Charge
Upgrade 1.Description:
Fly in the designated direction while cloaked in flame, attacking enemies in the path. This also increases the movement speed for a short time.

For a short time after this move hits, Talonflame's abilities and basic attacks partially ignore the enemies defense.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the user's Defense penetration for 3s and reduces the movement speed of opposing Pokémon in the user's path by 30% for 5s if this move hits.

Upgrade 2.Name: Aerial Ace
Upgrade 2.Description:
Fly at the designated enemy, dealing damage. Also makes the next basic attack a boosted attack.

Increased damage.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Move 2.Name: Acrobatics
Move 2.Description:
Fly toward the designated area and attack multiple times. Escape in a direction of your choice after using this move.

The user can choose to dash in the direction of movement afterward.

Upgrade 1.Name: Fly
Upgrade 1.Description:
Fly up into the sky. Using this move again will cause you to dive to the designated area and attack, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect. Your next basic attack becomes a boosted attack. This move's cooldown is reduced by 40% if it hits an enemy.

Also throws enemies when this move hits.

If this move hits a Pokémon from the opposing team, the current cooldown of this move is reduced by 40%.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.4s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Brave Bird
Upgrade 2.Description:
Dive to the designated location while cloaked in flame, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect. Also deals recoil damage to the user when it hits. Performing a boosted attack within a set amount of time after this move hits will reset the cooldown of Flame Charge or Aerial Ace.

Deals less recoil damage when this move hits.

Opposing Pokémon hit by this move are marked for 4s. If the user hits a marked target with a boosted attack, the cooldown of Flame Charge or Aerial Ace is reset and the mark on the first target hit is removed.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces damage received by 25% per opposing Pokémon hit for 5s (up to 3 times; maximum 75%).

Unite Move.Name: Flame Sweep
Unite Move.Description: Perform an aerial somersault before charging forawrd, dealing damage to enemies in this move's path and shoving them.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 80% Movement Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Shell Armor
Passive Ability.Description: If Lapras would take a single instance of damage that would be greater than 10% of Lapras's maximum HP, this damage will be reduced by 20%.

Lapras.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, shooting water through the target and dealing increased damage to Pokémon hit by the spray. The boosted attack also heals Lapras.

Move 1.Name: Whirlpool
Move 1.Description: Create a whirlpool centered on Lapras that lasts for 3s, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. The time between hits is shorter the farther opposing Pokémon are from the whirlpool’s center.

Upgrade 1.Name: Water Pulse
Upgrade 1.Description: Release a pulse of water centered on itself that spreads out, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s. For each opposing Pokémon hit by a pulse from this move, an additional pulse will be released the next time this move is used within 8 seconds, up to a total of 3 pulses. If the first pulse after activating this move hits a Pokémon, the user’s next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the maximum number of pulses to 4.

Upgrade 2.Name: Perish Song
