Upgrade 2.Description: Sing an eerie melody that echoes around yourself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. This move can be used up to three times consecutively within 5s of its last use. Pokémon hit all three times take additional damage equal to one Perish Song hit. If the additional damage would bring an enemy Pokémon below 10% of their max HP, that enemy Pokémon will take 40% of their maximum HP as True Damage.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: The next basic attack after using this move becomes a boosted attack.

Move 2.Name: Ice Shard
Move 2.Description: Hurl 5 chunks of ice in a forward direction, dealing damage to Pokémon they hit and leaving them frozen for 0.6s. If this move hits a Pokémon that is already frozen, it deals damage to it and shoves in the direction of the ice shards for 0.4s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Bubble Beam
Upgrade 1.Description: Create a bubble shield on Lapras for up to 4s or until the shield is broken, then spray 5 bubbles with a beam in the designated direction. Pokémon hit by the bubbles take damage and are enveloped by it, leaving them unable to act for 1s. After the bubbles fly a set distance, they slowly float for 3s. Pokémon hit by a floating bubble are enveloped by it and left unable to act for 1s, but will not be damaged.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the strength of the shield granted by this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Ice Beam
Upgrade 2.Description: Unleash an icy-cold beam of energy in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon hit and freezing the ground for 3s. Pokémon on the frozen ground take damage equal to the beam's damage every 0.4s they remain on it and have their movement speed decreased by 15% for 2s stacking up to 6 times. Pokémon become frozen for 1s if they remain on the frozen ground for 3 ticks. When Lapras is on the frozen ground, Lapras's movement speed is increased by 20%, lingering for 0.5s if Lapras leaves the frozen ground.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increase the duration Pokémon are Frozen for to 1.5s.

Unite Move.Name: Lapras Express
Unite Move.Description: Ride a wave quickly and continuously forward becoming Unstoppable for up to 15s. Lapras’s movement speed increases every second while riding but decreases if the user hits an obstacle. If this move is used again, the user stops and launches a wave that continues moving forward, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits and throwing them for 1.5s. If the user hits an enemy Pokémon while still riding, the user stops in place, dealing damage to and throwing the opposing Pokémon for 1.5s. While the user is riding the wave, one nearby ally Pokémon of the user’s choice within range may be picked up and can ride on its back. Ally Pokémon riding on the user’s back can dismount whenever they choose.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 20% Max HP Shield on activation, 40% Max HP shield and 30% Movement speed after stopping.

Passive Ability.Name: Clear Body
Passive Ability.Description: Reduces the duration of debuff effects inflicted on Dragapult by 50%

Dragapult.Attack:
Each time damage is dealt to enemies, boosted basic attack gauge increases. When the boosted basic attack gauge is full (after 8 instances of damage), the Pokémon's basic attacks become boosted attacks.

Each time the user deals damage to opposing Pokémon, the boosted attack gauge increases by 1, becoming full when the user deals damage 8 times. After not dealing damage for 3s, the boosted attack gauge decreases by 2 every 0.5s.

Move 1.Name: Astonish
Move 1.Description: Lets out a sound blast in a forward direction. The shout explodes when it hits an enemy or after traveling a set distance, dealing damage in the area and decreasing their movement speed for a short time

Upgrade 1.Name: Dragon Breath
Upgrade 1.Description:
Exhales a mighty gust in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed. The gust also leaves the ground burning for a set amount of time. Pokémon in this area of effect take damage over tim and have their movement speed decreased for a short time. If this move is used while the uesr is flying with Dragon Dance, the mighty gust is exhaled along Dragon Dance's flight path, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and the decreasing their movement speed for a short time.

Deals increased damage and further decreases their movement speed

If this move is used while the user is flying with Dragon Dance, the mighty gust is exhaled along Dragon Dance's flight path instead, with the same effects; the burning ground can hit up to 9 times.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move and the burning ground; strengthens the movement speed decrease to 45%.

Upgrade 2.Name: Shadow Ball
Upgrade 2.Description:
Hurls a shadowy blob in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon it touches and applying a mark to them. When marked Pokémon are hit by Dragapult, they receive additional damage. The fourth time the marked Pokémon is hit, the mark is removed, but the damage dealt by the attack is further increased and the user recovers HP. After using this move, the user moves a short distance in a designated direction.

Increased Damage
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move and the additional damage from marks.

Move 2.Name: Quick Attack
Move 2.Description: Dashes in the designated direction, increasing its basic attack speed for a short time

Upgrade 1.Name: Dragon Dance
Upgrade 1.Description:
Flies in a circular pattern in the designated direction. While flying, it continuously deals damage to nearby enemies with basic attacks that deal less damage but have increased attack speed. If this move is used again while the user is flying, the user stops flying and moves a short distance in the designated direction. If the user knocks out an enemy, this move's cooldown is reset. If the user assists in a knock out, this move's cooldown is reduced by half. When this move is used, Dragon's Breath's cooldown is reset

While the user is flying, its attacks also restores HP when they hit

When this move is used, Dragon Breath's cooldown is reset.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: While the user is flying, auto attacks restore the user's HP for an additional 25% of damage dealt.

Upgrade 2.Name: Phantom Force
Upgrade 2.Description:
For a short time, increases the user's movement speed and enters stealth. While the user is in stealth, the boosted basic attakck gauge increases. When stealth ends, Dragapult's basic attack speed is increased for a short time. After this move is learned, if the user knocks out an enemy, this move's cooldown is reset and Dragapult's Attack is increased by 6 until the end of battle. This Attack increase can stack up to 8 times.

Reduces this move's cooldown by 0.5s when the user deals damage to enemies with basic attacks.

When this move is learned, if the user knocks out a Pokémon from the opposing team, this move's cooldown is reset and the user's Attack is increased by 8 until the end of the battle (up to 10 times; maximum 80 bonus Attack).
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 0.5s per opposing Pokémon hit by the user's auto attacks.

Unite Move.Name: Dreep and Destroy
Unite Move.Description:
Launches two Dreepy and each deals damage to different Pokémon in the area of effect. The lower the opposing Pokémon's remaining HP is, the more damage the Dreepy deal. If there is only one target in the area of effect, both Dreepy target that Pokémon and deal damage to it. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve for this move. Dreepy may be fired once every 2 seconds. Neither Cooldown Reduction nor gaining Aeos Energy reduce the cooldown of Dreep and Destroy.

Against wild Pokémon: additional missing HP damage is capped at 1200.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 10% Max HP Shield, 10% Movement Speed for 3s; Has an internal cooldown of 30s to activate Energy Amplifier; Has an internal cooldown of 60s to activate Buddy Barrier;

Passive Ability.Name: Anticipation
Passive Ability.Description: When Eevee would be affected by a hindrance, the hindrance is negated and Eevee becomes immune to hindrances for a short time (35s cd). Note: Hindrances include slows; multi-layered hindrance moves such as Pikachu's Volt Tackle will only have the very first launch negated.
Passive Ability.Description 2: When Espeon would be affected by a hindrance, the hindrance is negated and Espeon becomes immune to hindrances for a short time. At the same time, this ability deals damage to the enemy that attempted to inflict the hindrance and applies a 30% slow for 1s (35s cd). Note: Hindrances include slows; multi-layered hindrance moves such as Pikachu's Volt Tackle will only have the very first launch negated.

Espeon.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

Move 1.Name: Swift
Move 1.Description: Eevee launches three stars at target location. Stars start in a horizontal line perpendicular to Eevee's facing. Stars continue to fly in the direction once past the target location until the maximum range is reached. When an enemy is hit by a star, subsequent stars deal 70% less damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Psyshock
Upgrade 1.Description: Espeon materializes psychic projectiles and launches them at a designated location; dealing damage to enemies hit by the projectiles and decreasing their movement speed for each projectile hit by 15% for 3s (maximum 75%). Orbs start in a horizontal line perpendicular to Espeon's facing and continue flying in the  designated direction until the maximum range of the move. Targets hit by all five projectiles are stunned for 1.25s. When an enemy is hit by a projectile, subsequent projectiles deal 70% less damage. When Psyshock is enhanced via Future Sight reset, it launches stronger psychic projectiles at a designated location. These projectiles at their point of convergence cause an explosion, dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. There is no damage increase.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increase the speed at which projectiles travel.

Upgrade 2.Name: Stored Power
Upgrade 2.Description: Unleash blasts of psychic power at random enemies within an area; dealing damage to enemies hit and decreasing their movement speed from each projectile hit by 10% for 2s (maximum 50%). Espeon stores one blast of psychic power every 2s (unaffected by cooldown reduction), or after hitting an enemy with a boosted attack (gaining 1 stored blast per enemy hit). Up to five blasts can be stored at once. After this move is used, Espeon's movement speed is increased by 70% for 1.2s. Targets hit by a psychic blast will take 70% less damage from subsequent hits. When Stored Power is enhanced via Future Sight reset, it unleashes an increased number of psychic blasts (8 total) and restores Espeon's HP by 150 per blast.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Also deals additional damage to enemies below 50% max HP.

Move 2.Name: Growl
Move 2.Description: Growl at a designated enemy; dealing damage and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Psybeam
Upgrade 1.Description:
Fires a peculiar ray in the designated direction. If the beam hits an enemy, it will then split beyond the first enemy hit and deal damage to other enemies in range. The initial enemy hit and these split rays deal more damage based on the max HP of the first enemy hit by the ray. If the first enemy hit by the ray is slowed or is stunned, those same effects will be applied to the enemies hit by the split rays. The split rays will never go towards Espeon and will refract at a maximum of a 90 degree angle. Distance from initial target to split ray hit is 75% of the max range. Stun copied duration is the maximum duration of the stun causing the initial Pokémon to be stunned.

For the split ray, the damage component from the initial target's max HP damage is capped at 1000 against Wild Pokémon and capped at 2000 against Player Pokémon.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Deals additional damage to enemies that are unable to act. If Psybeam hits an unable to act initial target, the split rays will also deal the additional damage to other enemies hit and leaves them unable to act.

Upgrade 2.Name: Future Sight
Upgrade 2.Description:
Espeon locks-on to an enemy, dealing damage 2 seconds later in an area centered on the locked-on target. Deal more damage based on the locked-on targets missing HP. While locked-on to an enemy, Espeon's other moves will target that enemy whenever possible. If the center of Psyshock is within the area-of-effect of Future Sight when released, Psyshock will home in on the Future Sight affected target. Each time Espeon deals damage to a Future Sight locked-on target through Psyshock, Stored Power, auto attacks, Future Sight's cooldown is reduced by 0.8 seconds. If the locked-on target is an opponent, and is knocked out by Future Sight or by Espeon, then a single powered-up use of Psyshock or Stored Power becomes available for a short time.

Full Heal and other cleanses such as Blissey's Natural cure remove Future Sight. Against Wild Pokémon: The total damage is capped at 1800.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Espeon deals 15% additional damage to locked-on targets.

Unite Move.Name: Psychic Solare
Unite Move.Description: Espeon throws enemies in the area of effect into the air and suspends them using psychic power. After a delay, an explosion occurs that deals damage to all enemies in the area of effect and shoves them. Espeon is unstoppable during this move.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Run Away
Passive Ability.Description: Eevee has 10% increased movement speed when out of combat. (5s after leaving combat)
Passive Ability.Description 2: While Leafeon is not in tall grass, the Chlorophyll gauge charges over 9s. When the gauge is full, Leafeon's movement speed is increased by 15%.

Leafeon.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing additional damage.

Move 1.Name: Swift
Move 1.Description: Launches three stars in the designated direction in a cone, dealing damage to enemies hit. The first star that hits applies a debuff that lasts 1 second that causes subsequent stars to deal 70% reduced damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Razor Leaf
Upgrade 1.Description: Launches a fan of five sharp-edged leaves in the designated direction in a cone, dealing damage to enemies hit before sticking into the ground for 2.5s. If this move is activated again, the leaves return, dealing damage to Pokémon they hit along the way. If the reactivation time is 1s or longer, the leaves will return and deal increased damage. If the move is not reactivated, the leaves will automatically return dealing increased damage. If five leaves hit the same Pokémon (outgoing or incoming) within 0.5s, Leafeon is granted a shield lasting 1.5s. Leafeon also receives 20% increased movement speed for up to 3s while the leaves are out. Each fan, the first leaf that hits applies a debuff that lasts 1s and causes subsequent leaves within the same fan (outgoing or incoming) to deal 70% reduced damage.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increase the strength of the shield when five leaves hit.

Upgrade 2.Name: Solar Blade
Upgrade 2.Description: Start gathering light and filling a blade with the light's energy. If this move is used again after light has been gathered, Leafeon brings the blade down in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits. The longer the blade is filled with light energy, the more damage is dealt. If this move is used when the Chlorophyll gauge is full, the blade is brought down immediately, dealing maximum damage and the cooldown for the next use of this move is reduced by 50%. The cooldown of this move is also reduced by 50% upon the Chlorophyll gauge being filled.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: While charging, Leafeon receives 50% damage reduction.

Move 2.Name: Quick Attack
Move 2.Description: Dash in a direction, dealing damage to enemies hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Aerial Ace
Upgrade 1.Description: Dash in a direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. The next attack is a boosted attack non-Chlorophyll empowered. A maximum of two uses may be kept in reserve for this move and cannot be used more than once every 0.5s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces the cooldown for charges by 0.5s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Leaf Blade
Upgrade 2.Description: Dash and spin in a direction, slashing around itself and with a ring of sharp leaves, dealing damage three times. Enemies caught by the outside edge of the ring of sharp leaves take increased damage and are slowed by 40% for 2s. Enemies damaged by the outside edge also charge the Chlorophyll gauge by 1.2s per hit.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1.2s for each hit dealing damage to more than one enemy on the outer ring

Unite Move.Name: Emerald Two-Step
Unite Move.Description: Leaps at a designated enemy, dealing damage to them and all enemies around them. These Pokémon are additionally slowed by 50% for 2s. After the initial leap, Leafeon leaps up and lands with force dealing damage to enemies in an area. Enemies hit by this area take more damage the closer they are to the center of this area. Leafeon is unstoppable during this move.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 80% Movement Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Run Away
Passive Ability.Description: When Eevee would be affected by a hindrance, the hindrance is negated and becomes invincible for 1s. This Ability goes on cooldown after it’s triggered (70s cd).
Passive Ability.Description 2: When Glaceon would be affected by a hindrance, the hindrance is negated. Glaceon then starts sneaking and becomes invincible for 0.5s. This Ability goes on cooldown after it’s triggered (70s cd) and is reset after respawning.

Glaceon.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage and giving Glaceon 2 ice crystals when the boosted attack hits. Ice crystals last 5s and the duration is refreshed upon gaining additional crystals. Glaceon can hold a maximum of 8 ice crystals at a time.

Move 1.Name: Swift
Move 1.Description: Creates 4 stars around the user. For a short time afterward, each time the user hits an enemy with a basic attack, one of the stars (or 2 stars in the case of a boosted attack) will shoot out and deal additional damage to that enemy.

Upgrade 1.Name: Icicle Spear
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user launch all of their stored ice crystals in rapid succession at the designated enemy. If no ice crystals are stored when this move is used, 2 ice crystals are instantly created and all crystals are launched. The crystals shatter when they hit the designated target, dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. Launched ice crystals can hit enemies on the opposing team that are not the designated target of this move. If this occurs, the ice crystals will still shatter on impact and deal damage in an area in the same way. Ice crystals launched by this move deal increasing damage each time they hit the same enemy. After this move is used, the cooldown of Ice Shard or Freeze-Dry is reduced.

Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to enemies hit, equal to a set percentage of their max HP.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to enemies hit equal to 0.5% of their remaining HP.

Upgrade 2.Name: Icy Wind
Upgrade 2.Description:
Jump a short distance in the designated direction. Glaceon's next basic attack launches all stored ice crystals at an enemy, dealing damage to them. Glaceon then regains the same number of ice crystals that were launched. If Glaceon hits the same enemy with 1 or more ice crystals, that enemies movement speed is decreased for a short time. The more ice crystals that hit that target, the stronger this movement speed-decrease effect. If Glaceon has no ice crystals when this move is used, gain 2 ice crystals.

Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to the enemy hit, equal to a set percentage of their max HP.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Each launched ice crystal also deals additional damage to enemies hit equal to 1.5% of their remaining HP.

Move 2.Name: Tail Whip
Move 2.Description: The user wags their tail at an enemy, dealing damage and decreasing their Defense, Sp. Def, and movement speed for a short time. The user's next basic attack after using this move becomes a boosted attack.

Upgrade 1.Name: Ice Shard
Upgrade 1.Description:
Increase movement speed for a short time. Afterward, Glaceon's basic attack speed increases for a short time, and basic attacks change into attacks that hurl chunks of ice. These chunks of ice deal damage to enemies hit and decrease their movement speed for a short time. Every time a chunk of ice hits, the user gains 1 ice crystal. The user gains 2 ice crystals if this move is used while launching ice crystals with Icicle Spear, or if it's used after Icy Wind has been used, but before the user has launched all ice crystals with the next basic attack.

Also increases basic attack speed for the duration of this move's effect.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases basic attack speed further to 100% for 2.5s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Freeze Dry
Upgrade 2.Description:
Glaceon shoots out a freezing burst of air in a forward direction, increasing Sp. Atk for a short time. . This burst explodes upon hitting an enemy or after traveling a set distance; dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. For each enemy hit by the explosion, Glaceon gains 1 ice crystal. The explosion also leaves behind an ice zone. Enemies that enter this ice zone will have their movement speed decreased. If they remain in the ice zone for a set amount of time, they become frozen for a short time.

For each enemy hit by this move's explosions; gain 1 ice crystal.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: For each enemy hit by this move's explosions, Glaceon gains 1 additional ice crystal.

Unite Move.Name: Glacial Stage
Unite Move.Description: Leap up high, creating an ice zone in front of Glaceon that deals damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. While Glaceon is in the ice zone, movement speed is increased and Glaceon gains 1 ice crystal whenever a set amount of time passes. The movement speed of enemies in the ice zone is decreased.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 35% Attack Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Magic Guard
Passive Ability.Description: This Pokémon receives a shield when restoring an ally Pokémon's HP. The strength of this shield is proportional to the amount of HP restored.

Clefable.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

Move 1.Name: Heal Pulse
Move 1.Description: Has the user immediately restore HP to itself and nearby allies.

Upgrade 1.Name: Moonlight
Upgrade 1.Description:
Clefable continually restores HP to themself and nearby allies for a short time. Clefable's movement speed is increased while this move is active.

Widens this move's area-of-effect and increases the duration of its effects.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Widens this move’s area-of-effect and increases the duration of its effects by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Draining Kiss
Upgrade 2.Description: Clefable attacks nearby enemies with an energy-draining kiss. Each enemy hit by the kiss receives damage and releases an air kiss. These air kisses will travel to a nearby ally with the lowest HP and restore their HP. If no allies are nearby, the air kisses will return to the user and restore Clefable's HP instead.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Widens this move’s area-of-effect and increases the amount of HP restored to 200% of the damage dealt.

Move 2.Name: Disarming Voice
Move 2.Description: Has the user let out a charming cry, dealing damage to nearby enemies and decreasing their movement speed for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Gravity
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user create a gravity zone centered on itself that persists for a set amount of time. Inside the gravity zone, Clefable's auto attacks change and deal increased damage. Enemies in the zone have their movement speed decreased which becomes stronger whenever a set amount of time passes. These enemies are also unable to use dash moves. If an enemy attempts to enter the zone with a dash move, the dash move is stopped and that enemy is left unable to act for a short time.

Widens the area-of-effect and increases the duration of its effects.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Widens this move’s area-of-effect and increases the duration of its effects to 4s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Follow Me
Upgrade 2.Description: Clefable moves to the designated location while waving its finger receiving a 3s shield. Attention of enemies hit and enemies near the designated location are drawn. These drawn enemies are forced to approach the user and attack Clefable with their auto attacks for 2s.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases Clefable's Defense by 150 for 3s and Sp. Def by 100 for 3s when this move is used.

Unite Move.Name: Wonder Wish
Unite Move.Description:
Clefable waggles their finger, restoring HP to allies in the area-of-effect. The lower an ally's HP is, the more HP this move restores to that ally. Afterward, Clefable gets a random move that can be performed by using the Unite Move again within 10s.

Note: Some random moves will activate immediately.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Static
Passive Ability.Description:
Upon receiving damage: paralyzes all nearby opposing Pokémon (30s CD).

Paralysis from Static decreases opposing Pokémon movement speed by 30% and reduces auto attack speed by a flat 10% for 2s.

Alolan Raichu.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage and paralyzing the opposing Pokémon hit.

Paralysis from Pikachu's boosted attacks decreases opposing Pokémon movement speed by 30% and reduces auto attack speed by a flat 10% for 2s.

Move 1.Name: Thunder Shock
Move 1.Description:
Releases electricity at the designated opposing Pokémon; damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and paralyzing those hit.

Paralysis from this move decreases opposing Pokémon movement speed by 30% and reduces auto attack speed by a flat 10% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Stored Power
Upgrade 1.Description:
Increases auto attack speed by 35% and empowers the next 6 auto attacks for 5s.

Each empowered auto attack also launches a star-shaped electric blast at the target, dealing additional damage. This move goes on cooldown after all 6 electric blasts have been launched or after 5s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: The electric blasts also decrease the target's Sp. Def by 10% for 3s (up to 3 times; maximum 30%).

Upgrade 2.Name: Electro Ball
Upgrade 2.Description:
Hurls an electric orb toward the designated location, where it remains for up to 30s if it doesn't hit an opposing Pokémon on the way. When an electric orb and an opposing Pokémon touch; the orb releases its electricity and damages nearby opposing Pokémon.

A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve, with a 0.5s cooldown between uses. A maximum of 3 orbs can be on the map at a given time.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Deals damage every 0.5s over 3.5s (8 times) to opposing Pokémon hit by this move.

Move 2.Name: Thunder Wave
Move 2.Description:
Discharges a weak jolt of electricity in the designated direction; damaging nearby opposing Pokémon and paralyzing those hit.

Paralysis from this move decreases opposing Pokémon movement speed by 30% and reduces auto attack speed by a flat 10% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Thunderbolt
Upgrade 1.Description:
Channels for 0.5s then releases a strong electric blast in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect, and paralyzing them when it hits. Paralysis from this move decreases opposing Pokémon movement speed by 30% and reduces auto attack speed by a flat 10% for 2s.

If this move hits an electric orb created by Electro Ball; the orb releases its electricity, dealing increased damage to nearby opposing Pokémon.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Immobilizes opposing Pokémon hit for 1s instead of paralyzing them.

Upgrade 2.Name: Psychic
Upgrade 2.Description:
Releases a strong telekinetic force in the designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and applying a debuff which decreases their movement speed by 23% for 3s when it hits. If the user hits debuffed opposing Pokémon with an auto attack, it deals additional damage.

If this move hits an electric orb created by Electro Ball; the orb is pulled to the center of this move's area of effect. This does not extend the duration the electric orb remains.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Creates a damage-over-time area of effect, damaging opposing Pokémon inside the area every 0.5s over 2s (up to 5 times).

Unite Move.Name: Thunderstorm Aerial
Unite Move.Description:
Creates a star-shaped zone of electricity in the designated area for 4s, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect when created, then damaging opposing Pokémon over time (up to 5 times) and decreasing their movement speed by 60% while in the zone.

If the user hits an opposing Pokémon that is in the zone with an auto attack, the next basic attack becomes a boosted attack.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 35% Attack Speed, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Static
Passive Ability.Description:
Paralyzes all enemies nearby for a short time when receiving damage. This ability goes on cooldown after it's triggered.

The movement speed of opposing Pokémon paralyzed by this Ability is decreased by 30% for 2.5s and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 2s.

Pikachu.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage. This boosted attack also paralyzes enemies for a short time when it hits.

The movement speed of the opposing Pokémon paralyzed by boosted attacks is decreased by 30% and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 1s.

Move 1.Name: Thunder Shock
Move 1.Description:
Release electricity, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect. Also paralyzes enemies for a short time.

The movement speed of opposing Pokémon paralyzed by this move is decreased by 30% and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Electro Ball
Upgrade 1.Description:
Hurl an electric orb, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them paralyzed for a short time. This move's damage increases the lower the enemies remaining HP is.

Increased damage.

The movement speed of opposing Pokémon paralyzed by this move is decreased by 50% and their basic attack speed is decreased by 10% for 2s. Against wild Pokémon: additional missing HP damage is capped at 1200.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Upgrade 2.Name: Thunder
Upgrade 2.Description:
Drop wicked thunderbolts, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect.

Increases the number of times this move attacks.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Deals damage over time more frequently, hitting opposing Pokémon in the area of effect up to 7 times over 2.5s.

Move 2.Name: Electroweb
Move 2.Description: Attacks and captures enemies using an electric net, leaving them unable to move.

Upgrade 1.Name: Volt Tackle
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user electrify itself and charge ahead, throwing opposing Pokémon.

Reduced cooldown.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Thunderbolt
Upgrade 2.Description:
Attack with a strong electric blast, dealing damage to enemies in the area of effect and leaving them unable to act.

Increased damage.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Unite Move.Name: Thunderstorm
Unite Move.Description:
Attacks enemies near the user with tremendous bolts of lightning.

Pikachu can act freely once the first bolts of lightning are cast down.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 30% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Pressure
Passive Ability.Description:
When Mewtwo deals damage to enemy players with a basic attack, the Mega gauge increases up to 12 times. As the Mega gauge increases, Mewtwo's Sp. Atk is increased by 1.5%, and attack speed is increased by 3%, up to 10 times. If Mewtwo is level 5 or higher, and the Mega gauge has increased at least 12 times, Mewtwo Mega Evolves. The Mega Evolution lasts 10s and increases Sp. Atk by an additional 10%, attack speed by an additional 35%, max HP by 10%, and movement speed by 5%.

Consumes up to 30 Aeos Energy upon Mega Evolving. Mega Evolution duration is extended by 1 second for every 5 energy consumed. Energy is consumed regardless if you have the next multiple of 5. The Mega gauge does not deplete upon being KO'd.

Mewtwo Y.Attack: When Mewtwo Mega Evolves, attacks have increased range and deal damage in an area-of-effect centered on the target. When an attack deals damage to an enemy, their movement speed is decreased.

Move 1.Name: Confusion
Move 1.Description: Deal damage to an enemy and leave them unable to act for 1s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Future Sight
Upgrade 1.Description:
Mewtwo shoves the designated enemy, dealing damage, leaving them unable to act for 0.5s and locking onto them. The locked-on enemy has their movement speed decreased by 35% for 3s and takes 10% increased damage from Mewtwo. After 3s, Mewtwo deals damage to the locked-on enemy a second time, and additional damage equal to 50% of any damage they received while locked-on.

Additional damage is capped at 1500.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: The locked-on enemy instead takes 15% increased damage.

Upgrade 2.Name: Psystrike
Upgrade 2.Description:
Mewtwo becomes unstoppable and starts directing psychic waves at the designated enemy, each hit dealing damage and decreasing their movement speed by 10% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times. Psystrike damage is increased based on distance from the target enemy; dealing up to 40% increased damage when Mewtwo is attacking from slightly beyond its basic attack range. At the end of the move, Mewtwo sends a final hit, dealing damage to the designated enemy and nearby enemies then throws them for 0.6s.

If Psystrike is intercepted by an enemy that isn't the intended target, there's no explosion and the enemy hit is shoved instead.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Movement speed reduction on enemies is increased to 15% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times.

Move 2.Name: Barrier
Move 2.Description: Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, its movement speed is increased by 20%.

Upgrade 1.Name: Recover
Upgrade 1.Description: Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, movement speed is increased by 30%, and Mewtwo recovers HP equal to 40% of the damage dealt from Basic Attacks. If at the end of the 3s the shield hasn't been destroyed, all of the user's move cooldowns are reduced by 25% of their current cooldown, and the Mega gauge increases once.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the amount of HP recovered to 50% of damage dealt with Basic Attacks.

Upgrade 2.Name: Teleport
Upgrade 2.Description: Mewtwo teleports to the designated location. For 2s after the first cast of Teleport, movement speed is increased by 15%, Mewtwo deals 20% more damage, and the Mega gauge increases 50% faster. During those 2s, Mewtwo can teleport again once.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Damage dealt is increased to 30%.

Unite Move.Name: Infinite Psyburn
Unite Move.Description: Mewtwo becomes unstoppable while revealing enemy players and channeling a powerful attack on the enemies' current position. After 1s, Mewtwo brings down a powerful psychic beam on their original position, dealing damage to enemies around each point of impact, leaving them unable to act for 0.6s and decreasing their Sp. Defense by 15% for 3s. Each Pokémon can only be hit by 1 psychic beam.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Flash Fire
Passive Ability.Description: When this Pokémon takes Sp. Atk-based damage from opposing Pokémon, the damage this Pokémon takes is reduced by 20% for 3s, and their next auto attack deals additional damage. This Ability goes on a 10s cooldown after it ends.

Armarouge.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage.

Move 1.Name: Incinerate
Move 1.Description: Has the user attack the designated opposing Pokémon with a flame, setting them ablaze and dealing damage over time for 4s. When a Pokémon on the opposing team that's ablaze picks up a Berry, the Berry's effects are halved.

Upgrade 1.Name: Fire Spin
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user generate a fierce vortex of fire around themself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit every 0.5s. Each time Armarouge deals damage while Fire Spin is active, gain a shield. This shield can stack up to six times.

Gain 20% movement speed while the vortex is active. The vortex lasts 3s, and its duration is increased by 1s if the user hits opposing Pokémon with an auto attack. This extended duration can stack up to 3 times, to a total vortex duration of 6s. Moving towards an enemy will additionally increase movement speed by 10%.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Upon using Fire Spin+, gain 3 stacks of shields.

Upgrade 2.Name: Armor Cannon
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user combine their shoulder armor and start charging power. When charged past a minimum duration, the user then fires a blazing ball of flame. The user releases a shock wave in front of themself when firing, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in close range.

The fired ball of flame explodes when it hits an opposing Pokémon or after it travels a set distance, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and leaving them unable to act for 0.45s. The user's Defense and Sp. Def are decreased by 5% while this move is on cooldown.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the range of this move and auto attacks.

Move 2.Name: Will-o-Wisp
Move 2.Description: Has the user shoot a flame in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit, leaving them burned and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s. After using this move, the user's next auto attack becomes a boosted attack.

Upgrade 1.Name: Flame Charge
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user cloak themself in flame and charge in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon the user hits and shoving them for 0.45s. When this move hits an opposing Pokémon, the user is granted a shield for 3s and this move's cooldown is reduced by 20%.

For 3s after using this move, the cannon gauge becomes full and will not decrease after boosted attacks are used.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces the damage the user receives by 20% for 3s after this move hits an opposing Pokémon.

Upgrade 2.Name: Psyshock
Upgrade 2.Description:
Has the user materialize odd psychic waves to attack in three areas of effect in front of themself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit while ignoring 35% of their Sp. Def. The third attack throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.7s. Multiple of the areas of effect can hit the same opposing Pokémon but the second damage instance will be reduced by 25% and the third by 50%.

For 3s after using this move, the cannon gauge becomes full and will not decrease after boosted attacks are used.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases this move's area of effect for the third area blast.

Unite Move.Name: Psykaboom
Unite Move.Description: Has the user jump up, becoming unstoppable for 1.85s, and charge psychic power for 1s, then create an area that leaves opposing Pokémon unable to act. While the user is charging psychic power, the Unite Move can be used again to adjust the position of the area. The user then fires a ball of flame towards the center of the area, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and throwing them away from the center of the area.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Infiltrator
Passive Ability.Description: When this Pokémon deals damage, receive a stacking buff that makes Special damage dealt by this Pokémon ignore 2.5% of Special Defense per stack. This buff lasts 4s, can stack up to 8 times, and the duration refreshes when gaining a new stack.

Chandelure.Attack: Gains a Boosted Attack every 5s, dealing increased damage, slowing enemies by 30% for 2s and burning enemies hit for 2.5s.

Move 1.Name: Ember
Move 1.Description:
Shoot out a small flame, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits, slowing them by 30% for 2s and leaving them burned for 2.5s. After the first cast, this move may be used again within 5s before going on full cooldown.

Burns tick every 0.5s for 5 damage ticks.

Upgrade 1.Name: Flamethrower
Upgrade 1.Description:
Shoot an intense blast of fire in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon and leaving them burned for 2.5s. The end of the blast explodes, dealing increased damage. If the explosion hits a Pokémon, this move's cooldown is reduced by 65% of its current cooldown.

Burns tick every 0.5s for 5 damage ticks.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: If the explosion hits a Pokémon, Chandelure deals 20% increased damage for 3s. This effect also applies to the triggering explosion itself.

Upgrade 2.Name: Overheat
Upgrade 2.Description:
Throws consecutive flames in an arc into designated areas up to 3 times by holding the move input down. Pokémon in the area are hit and are left burned. The area may be aimed and changed during the use of this move. When one of the flames hits a Pokémon, the user gains one heat energy. If this move is used again, an exploding flame is shot out, dealing damage to Pokémon based on the heat energy and leaves them burned. The exploding flame hitting with 0, 1 or 2 heat energy reduces the cooldown of this move by 75%, 50% or 30% respectively. After the follow-up attack, the user cannot use basic attacks for a short time.

Burns tick every 0.5s for 5 damage ticks.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: This move's cooldown is reduced by 1s.

Move 2.Name: Night Shade
Move 2.Description: Makes Pokémon in the area of effect see a frightening mirage, dealing damage to them and marking them. When Chandelure deals damage again to the marked Pokémon, the marked Pokémon are stunned for 1s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Poltergeist
Upgrade 1.Description: Create a zone of mysterious power and mirages of floating furniture.  Pokémon that come in contact with a mirage take damage.  Pokémon that come in contact with a mirage or the edge of the zone are slowed by 65% decaying to a 40% slow over 1s. Then the 40% slow lingers for an additional second.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the slow to 80% for 1s decaying to 50% for 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Imprison
Upgrade 2.Description: Creates a zone in front of itself lasting 4s. The first enemy caught in the zone will be immobilized for 0.7s. Furthermore, all enemy Pokémon in the zone or enter the zone become imprisoned and cannot use Moves.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Changes the Imprison to briefly immobilize all Pokémon caught in the zone on creation.

Unite Move.Name: Ignite Midnight
Unite Move.Description: Reduce the vision of enemies around itself by 30% for 3s. If this Unite Move is used again, Chandelure continuously fires a beam of fire in a designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits. The beam may be redirected while firing and its final blast deals increased damage. Ignite Midnight can be cancelled early.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 40% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Prankster
Passive Ability.Description: When Sableye is outside the visual range of Pokémon from the opposing team, it enters stealth and its movement speed increases. When Sableye enters the visual range of a Pokémon from the opposing team, it does not immediately become visible but instead remains in stealth for a short time. This stealth lasts longer when approaching enemy Pokémon from behind. The Pokémon with this Ability exits stealth when it attacks.

Sableye.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. If Sableye is in stealth when it hits an opposing Pokémon with a boosted attack, it will deal damage and apply a fear effect. Opposing Pokémon affected by fear will be forced to move away from Sableye. When the user enters stealth, it will charge boosted attack immediately and hold it.

Move 1.Name: Thief
Move 1.Description: Dashes in the designated direction, dealing damage to any Pokémon it hits and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. If this move hits an enemy Pokémon, it steals a small amount of their Aeos energy. If those Pokémon do not have any Aeos energy, they will be left unable to act for a short time.

Upgrade 1.Name: Knock Off
Upgrade 1.Description:
Dashes in the designated direction and then back again while attacking around itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon hit by the attacks and leaving them unable to act for a short time. For a short time afterward, this move can be used again. If it is, the user attacks in front of itself with its claws, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits and throwing them. When either the first or second stage of this move hits opposing Pokémon, those Pokémon will drop some of their collected Aeos energy, and for a short time, they’ll be unable to pick up Aeos energy. When either stage of this move hits opposing Pokémon that do not have Aeos energy, those Pokémon will be left unable to act—and for a longer duration. While using this move, the user receives reduced damage.

For a short time after this move is used, the next time the user picks up Aeos energy, the user recovers HP and has its movement speed increased.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Aeos energy picked up within 5s of using this move recovers 9% Max HP (one time per activation) and increases movement speed by 40% for 2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Shadow Sneak
Upgrade 2.Description:
For a short time, increases the user’s movement speed and has it enter stealth. While this move is active, the user’s next boosted basic attack deals additional damage. While this move is active, if the user exits stealth and is not immobilized by a hindrance, it will be able to enter stealth again.

Increases the duration of this move’s effects. While this move is active and the user is in stealth, it will also gradually recover HP.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the duration of this move’s effects to 7.5s. While this move is active and Sableye is in stealth, Sableye will also restore 1% max HP every 0.5s.

Move 2.Name: Astonish
Move 2.Description: Attacks in a cone in front of itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon and decreasing their movement speed for a short time. If this move hits opposing Pokémon from behind, it deals increased damage and applies a greater decrease in movement speed.

Upgrade 1.Name: Feint Attack
Upgrade 1.Description:
Has the user throw dummy Aeos energy to a designated location. While dummy Aeos energy remains at a location, that location and the area around it will be visible to the user. Dummy Aeos energy can remain at up to five locations at a time. If an opposing Pokémon picks up dummy Aeos energy, that energy explodes, dealing damage over time to that Pokémon and decreasing its movement speed for a short time. A maximum of three uses can be kept in reserve for this move.

If an opposing Pokémon is knocked out while taking damage over time from this move, it will drop more Aeos energy than it normally would.

Note: Placing the dummy energy does not break stealth. Stealth is only broken when an enemy takes damage from the dummy energy.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: If an enemy is knocked out while affected by this move, they will drop more Aeos energy than they normally would.

Upgrade 2.Name: Confuse Ray
Upgrade 2.Description:
Fires a sinister ray in the designated direction, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon it hits. If this move hits Pokémon from the opposing team, it also leaves them confused. While confused, Pokémon are left unable to act and start continuously attacking nearby Pokémon with basic attacks. Confused Pokémon will target nearby Pokémon in the following order of priority: first, their ally Pokémon; then, wild Pokémon; last, Pokémon on Sableye's team.

Also increases the movement speed and basic attack speed of Pokémon from the opposing team while this move has left them confused.

While confused, outgoing damage is decreased for auto attacks and move 1 by 50%. If the confused target knocks out a Wild Pokémon, Sableye receives the KO.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Also increases target's movement speed by 30% and attack speed by 50% while confused from this move.

Unite Move.Name: Chaos Glower
Unite Move.Description: Shoot light from both eyes, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in an cone in front of itself. Pokémon from the opposing team hit by this move who are facing the user are left unable to act and start preparing to return to base. If they take damage before returning to base, the return effect is cancelled, and they are left unable to act for a short time. Opposing Pokémon hit by this move who are not facing the user have their movement speed decreased for a short time.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Pressure
Passive Ability.Description:
When Mewtwo deals damage to an opposing Pokémon with a basic attack, the Mega gauge increases. Each time the Mega gauge increases, Mewtwo attack is increased by 2% and its Defense and Sp. Def are increased by 2%, up to 10 times. If Mewtwo is Lv. 5 or higher, and the Mega gauge has stacked at least 9 times, it Mega Evolves. The Mega Evolution lasts 15s and increases Mewtwo attack by an additional 10%, Defense and Sp. Def by an additional 10%, its max HP by 10% and its movement speed by 5%.

Consumes up to 30 Aeos Energy upon Mega Evolving. Mega Evolution duration is extended by 1 second for every 5 energy consumed. Energy is consumed regardless if you have the next multiple of 5. The Mega gauge does not deplete upon being KO'd.

Mewtwo X.Attack: When Mewtwo Mega Evolves, deal increased damage and becomes a boosted attack with every third attack.

Move 1.Name: Confusion
Move 1.Description: Deal damage to an enemy and leave them unable to act for 1s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Future Sight
Upgrade 1.Description:
Mewtwo pulls the designated enemy toward itself dealing damage, leaving them unable to act for 0.75s and locking on to them. The locked-on enemy has their movement speed decreased by 25% for 3s and takes 10% increased damage from Mewtwo. After 3s, Mewtwo deals damage to the locked-on enemy a second time, and additional damage equal to 50% of any damage they received while locked-on.

Against Enemy Players: Maximum damage is capped at 2000.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: The locked-on enemy instead takes 20% increased damage.

Upgrade 2.Name: Psystrike
Upgrade 2.Description:
Mewtwo becomes unstoppable and starts directing psychic waves at the designated enemy, each hit dealing damage and decreasing their movement speed by 10% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times. While Mewtwo is directing psychic waves, it takes 15% reduced damage. At the end of the move, Mewtwo sends a final hit, dealing damage to the designated enemy and nearby enemies then throws them for 0.6s.

If Psystrike is intercepted by an enemy that isn't the intended target, there's no explosion and the enemy hit is shoved instead.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Movement speed reduction on enemies is increased to 15% for 1s, stacking up to 5 times.

Move 2.Name: Barrier
Move 2.Description: Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, its movement speed is increased by 20%.

Upgrade 1.Name: Recover
Upgrade 1.Description: Mewtwo gains a shield for 3s. While the shield effect is active, movement speed is increased by 30%, and Mewtwo recovers HP equal to 20% of all incoming damage received from enemies while the shield is active. When Mewtwo takes enough damage for the shield effect to be removed, all of the user's move cooldowns are reduced by 25% of their current cooldown, and the Mega gauge increases twice.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the amount of HP recovered to 30% of incoming damage received.

Upgrade 2.Name: Teleport
Upgrade 2.Description: Mewtwo teleports to the designated location. For 5s, movement speed is increased by 30%, Mewtwo deals 10% more damage, and the Mega gauge increases 50% faster.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Damage dealt is increased to 20%.

Unite Move.Name: Infinite Psyburn
Unite Move.Description: Mewtwo becomes unstoppable while revealing enemy players and channeling a powerful attack on the enemies' current position. After 1s, Mewtwo brings down a powerful psychic beam on their original position, dealing damage to enemies around each point of impact, leaving them unable to act for 1s and decreasing their Defense by 20% for 3s. Each Pokémon can only be hit by 1 psychic beam.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: No Guard
Passive Ability.Description: Honedge and Doublade do not gain the stat benefits of Stance Change. However, their boosted attack functions like Aegislash's Sword Stance boosted attack.
Passive Ability.Description 2: Aegislash can switch between Sword Stance and Shield Stance. Using a blade move switches stance to Sword Stance; which increases Attack, movement speed by 5%, and Attack Speed by 15%. Using a shield move switches stance to Shield Stance; which increases Defense and Sp. Defense and reduces Attack Speed by 20%. After switching stances, movement speed and basic attack speed are further increased by 15% for 3.5s.

Aegislash.Attack:
Slash at enemies in front of Aegislash, dealing damage to them. If the boost count is one or more, basic attacks become a boosted attack. When in Blade Stance: pierce through enemies, dealing damage. When in Shield Stance: ram enemies, dealing damage and leaving them unable to act for a short time, restore HP, and reduce the cooldown of Sacred Sword and Shadow Claw when this attack hits. Every boosted attack consumes one boost count. Up to 4 boosted boosted attacks can be stored.

As a boosted attack in Sword Forme: pierces through the target, damaging opposing Pokémon hit. As a boosted attack in Shield Forme: slams into the target, damaging opposing Pokémon hit and stunning them for 0.1s; restores HP, and reduces the cooldown of Sacred Sword or Shadow Claw by 1s when it hits.

Move 1.Name: Shadow Sneak
Move 1.Description:
Cast a shadow in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. After the shadow hits an enemy or travels its maximum distance, Aegislash warps to the shadow’s location, dealing damage to nearby enemies and increase boost count by 1.

Increases the boost count by 1 if the warp hits an opposing Pokémon.

Upgrade 1.Name: Sacred Sword
Upgrade 1.Description:
Aegislash thrusts its blade into the ground, creating a triangular zone. This zone erupts with a hidden power, dealing damage to enemies in the area and throws them. Also increases boost count by 1 for each enemy hit (capped at 2 for wilds, 4 for players). At the same time as the eruption, Aegislash slashes forward, dealing additional damage to enemies hit. For a short time after this slash hits, damage dealt by Aegislash partially ignores the Defense of all enemies.

Increase Attack for a short time after hitting enemies with this move.

Increases the boost count by 1 for each target hit by the triangular zone erupting (up to 2 from hitting wild Pokémon).
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases Attack by 50 for 3s after the triangular zone hits.

Upgrade 2.Name: Shadow Claw
Upgrade 2.Description:
Aegislash slashes with its blade twice in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. Follow up with a final rising slash that deals damage to enemies hit and throw them. Boost count increases by 1 when at least one of the three slashes hits.

Increases Critical-Hit Rate with all attacks and moves except for Coup de Grace for a short time.

Increases the boost count by 1 if this move hits.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases critical-hit rate by 20% for 1s after the final slash.

Move 2.Name: Iron Defense
Move 2.Description:
Envelops Aegislash in a powerful protective force for 1s. The protection disappears after blocking one attack or after 1s and then increases boost count by 1.

Increases the boost count by 1 when this move blocks an attack.

Upgrade 1.Name: Wide Guard
Upgrade 1.Description:
Focus in place for a short time to create a great shield on Aegislash. When this shield appears, boost count increases by 1, granting a shield to Aegislash and shoves enemies in front. While this shield is active, enemy player attacks increase boost count by 1 for each different enemy player (capping at 4). When this move ends, shove enemies in front of Aegislash once again.

Cooldown reduced by 1s.

Increases the boost count by 1 unconditionally when granting the shield effect. Increases the boost count by 1 more for each instance of damage received by the shield effect (up to 2 times). Increases boost count by 1 more if an opposing Pokémon is shoved at the end or unconditionally when the shield effect is depleted before it can end normally.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Iron Head
Upgrade 2.Description:
Dash in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. Also increases boost count by 1, and protects Aegislash with a special shield for a short time afterward. This shield doesn’t protect from hindrances, but it can nullify damage. When the shield blocks an attack, it disappears and boost count is increased by 1.

Increase Attack for a short time after blocking an attack.

Increases the boost count by 1 unconditionally after barreling forward. Increases the boost count by 1 more if the shield blocks an attack.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases Attack by 10% for 3s when the special shield blocks an attack.

Unite Move.Name: Coup de Grace
Unite Move.Description:
Unleash a ground-splitting slash in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies hit. The lower their remaining HP, the more damage this move deals. If this slash knocks out an enemy, boost count increases by 1.

Increases the boost count by 2 if this Unite Move knocks out an opposing Pokémon.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Pressure
Passive Ability.Description:
Damaging a Pokémon from the opposing team below 10% max HP and frozen by Suicune deals an additional 15% max HP as true damage to the target (sufficient damage reduction can prevent the target from being knocked out). Using a move grants a shield for 5s (up to 2 times); separately increases the crystal gauge by 1 for 5s (becoming full when increased 2 times).

Flooded areas created by Whirlpool or Surf remain for 5s; opposing Pokémon in a flooded area are marked as drenched for 3s. Freezing a flooded area with a regular move (not Unite Move) also freezes opposing Pokémon in that flooded area for 1s. Freezing an opposing Pokémon marked as drenched removes the mark; targets frozen by a regular move or by a frozen area cannot be marked as drenched for 3s afterward. Deals bonus damage when freezing opposing Pokémon with a regular move or by being in a frozen area.

Suicune.Attack:
When the crystal gauge is full, readies a boosted attack for 5s as the crystal gauge decays to 0; increasing the crystal gauge refills it to full.

Boosted attacks shoot water forward, damaging opposing Pokémon every 0.25s over 1s (up to 4 times), dealing bonus damage to frozen opposing Pokémon with each hit.

Move 1.Name: Water Pulse
Move 1.Description:
Fires a pulse of water in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon and shoving them for 0.5s when it hits.

Marks targets as drenched for 3s when it hits.

Upgrade 1.Name: Whirlpool
Upgrade 1.Description:
Generates a vortex of water at the designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect every 1s over 4s (up to 4 times), pulling them toward the center with each hit, and slowing enemies by 20% for 0.5s (refreshing on each hit). If used again within 4s, moves the vortex in the designated direction, flooding the area it passes through.

Marks targets as drenched for 3s when hit.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Surf
Upgrade 2.Description:
Prepares an area for a big wave to pass through, then creates the wave and sends it in the designated direction after 0.75s, damaging opposing Pokémon hit by the wave and shoving them. The prepared area becomes flooded after the wave passes through it.

Marks targets hit by the wave as drenched for 3s. Hitting a pillar of ice created by Icy Wind with the wave shatters it, damaging opposing Pokémon hit.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage of this move.

Move 2.Name: Avalanche
Move 2.Description:
Creates snow to fall at the designated area for 1.5s, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect (up to 3 times) and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 2s when hit. After 1.5s, drops a clump of snow at the designated area, damaging opposing Pokémon again.

The clump of snow freezes drenched targets for 1s when it hits; freezes flooded areas it drops on after 1s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Ice Beam
Upgrade 1.Description:
Fires an icy-cold beam of energy in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon and decreasing their movement speed by 20% for 2s when it hits.

This move freezes drenched targets for 1s when it hits; freezes flooded areas it hits after 1s. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve, with a 0.5s cooldown between uses.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the movement speed decrease to 30%.

Upgrade 2.Name: Icy Wind
Upgrade 2.Description:
Builds up a gust of freezing air at the designated area for 0.5s, decreasing the movement speed of opposing Pokémon in the area of effect by 30% for 3s; then forms a pillar of ice, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and decreasing their movement speed by 30% for 3s once fully formed. The pillar of ice shatters after 4s, damaging opposing Pokémon again.

This move freezes drenched targets for 1s when the pillar of ice forms or when it shatters. Forming a pillar of ice on a flooded area freezes that area after 1s. A maximum of 2 uses can be kept in reserve, with a 0.5s cooldown between uses.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces this move's cooldown by 1s.

Unite Move.Name: Endless Ice Spikes
Unite Move.Description:
Cleanses the user of status condition; then creates ice spikes in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and freezing those hit for 2s. After 2s, the ice spikes explode, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect again. This Unite Move does not directly interact with drench marks, nor does it freeze flooded areas.

Suicune can be stunned after cleansing themself and before creating the ice spikes, canceling the Unite Move and refunding 90% of the Unite Move gauge.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Rattled
Passive Ability.Description:
When damaged, increases movement speed by 5% for 3s (up to 5 times; maximum 25%).

Increases the effort gauge each time Magikarp uses an auto attack or move, hits with an auto attack or move, scores a goal, knocks out a Pokémon from the opposing team or assists in knocking out a Pokémon from the opposing team. Once the effort gauge is full, Magikarp evolves into Gyarados.
Passive Ability.Description 2: When knocking out a Pokémon from the opposing team or making an assist, reduces current move cooldowns by 30%.

Gyarados.Attack:
Magikarp has no boosted attack.

Magikarp's basic attacks are a set animation length.

Move 1.Name: Flail
Move 1.Description: Flails on the spot, damaging nearby opposing Pokémon; damage strengthens for every 33% max HP the user is missing.

Upgrade 1.Name: Dragon Breath
Upgrade 1.Description: Exhales a mighty gust in the designated direction, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and paralyzing them for 3s when it hits, decreasing their movement speed and basic attack speed by 30%. If this move hits, gains a buff for 5s; increasing the range and damage of auto attacks; and increasing basic attack speed by a minimum of 30%, strengthening by 10% for each additional opposing Pokémon hit (up to 2 times; maximum 50%).
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Increases the damage dealt by this move and enhanced auto attacks.

Upgrade 2.Name: Aqua Tail
Upgrade 2.Description:
For up to 4s while this move is held; moves slightly in the designated direction then swings a blade of water from their tail in the designated direction (up to 5 times), damaging opposing Pokémon hit with each blade.

If at least 3 blades of water hit opposing Pokémon before this move is released or ends, a second stage of this move can be used within 5s; becomes unstoppable and charges for up to 1.5s, then attacks with a large sweep of their tail in a wider area forward, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area of effect. If the second stage is charged for at least 0.5s, increases the range of the area of effect and damage dealt.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: The boosted aqua tail deals an additional 20% missing HP damage to opposing Pokémon. Against wild Pokémon: additional missing HP damage is capped at 1000.

Move 2.Name: Splash
Move 2.Description:
Jumps to the designated location forward.

A maximum of 3 uses can be kept in reserve, with a 2s cooldown between uses.

Upgrade 1.Name: Waterfall
Upgrade 1.Description:
Charges forward up to 3 times, damaging opposing Pokémon hit with each charge; waiting for at least 0.07s before dashing forward with the next charge. The first charge is a regular dash forward; the next movement within 2s after the last charge dashes forward with the same effect. Dragon Breath or Aqua Tail can be used within the 2s duration in between charges without interrupting this move's course. Using Dragon Current ends this move early.

If two charges hit opposing Pokémon, grants a shield for 2s. If all three charges hit opposing Pokémon, the third charge throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.45s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Reduce this move's cooldown by 1s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Bounce
Upgrade 2.Description:
Becomes unstoppable and charges power for up to 2.4s, becoming fully charged after 0.8s; then bounces to the designated location forward, damaging opposing Pokémon in the area upon impact. While charging power, increases the range of this move, reaching maximum range when fully charged.

If fully charged when released, grants a shield for 5s, and throws opposing Pokémon hit for 0.45s. If this move is canceled, it goes on a 2s cooldown.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Strengthens the value of the shield effect.

Unite Move.Name: Dragon Current
Unite Move.Description:
Becomes untargetable and dives underwater for up to 4s while this Unite Move is held, increasing movement speed by 45%. When released or after 4s, forcefully leaps up and attacks, damaging opposing Pokémon hit. The longer the move is held, the larger the area of effect becomes.

If the user stays underwater for at least 1s before releasing the Unite Move, strengthens the movement speed increase to 55%; deals increased damage and creates a whirlpool where they surface, dealing damage over time to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect (up to 5 times) and decreasing their movement speed for 4s when hit. If underwater for at least 2s, strengthens the movement speed increase to 65%; deals even more damage and creates a waterspout where they surface instead, throwing opposing Pokémon hit for 1s.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Clear Body
Passive Ability.Description: Reduces the duration of hindrance effects by 10% when enemy players are nearby. The more enemy players there are, the more the hindrance effects are reduced, up to a maximum of 5 enemy players (50% max).

Metagross.Attack: Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, striking with an increased area of attack. When a boosted attack hits, the cooldown of Iron Defense, Meteor Mash or Gyro Ball is reduced by 10%. If multiple targets are hit, the cooldown is instead reduced by 20%.

Move 1.Name: Iron Defense
Move 1.Description: Gain a shield lasting 5s and gain a boosted attack. That boosted attack deals additional damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Meteor Mash
Upgrade 1.Description: The user charges power in their fist, dealing damage to enemies in an area up to 4x and slowing them by 20% for 3s while granting the user a shield for 5s. Enemies may dodge the charging damage by moving out of the area. The more enemies that this move hits, the more shield the user gains (stacking up to 8 times) and the more damage is dealt with the next basic attack. The next basic attack hits with a hard punch fired like a meteor, dealing damage to enemies in an area of effect. The level of charge depends on how many hits were made during the charging.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Restores HP when the punch hits enemies.

Upgrade 2.Name: Gyro Ball
Upgrade 2.Description: Spin rapidly, dealing damage to nearby enemies. Hitting enemies with the outer edge of the spin grants a shield to the user for 4s. The shield stacks based on the number of targets hit up to a maximum of 6 times, with wild Pokémon granting 1 stack and enemy players granting 3 stacks.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: On expiration of shield duration, converts 30% of the remaining shield to healing.

Move 2.Name: Tackle
Move 2.Description: Scan in a designated direction. If the scan hits an enemy, the user then charges toward the enemy hit, dealing damage.

Upgrade 1.Name: Zen Headbutt
Upgrade 1.Description: Fires a psychic wave in a designated direction. If the move hits an enemy, the user then charges toward the enemy hit, dealing damage and throwing them for 0.8s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: After dealing damage with Zen Headbutt, reduce incoming damage by 20% for 2s.

Upgrade 2.Name: Magnet Rise
Upgrade 2.Description: Levitates for up to 4s using electrically-generated magnetism, increasing the user's movement speed by 60% diminishing by 15% every second. If a basic attack is made while levitating, the user will fall to the ground forcefully, dealing damage to nearby enemies and knocking them up for 0.6s. This basic attack can lunge at a target crossing terrain and obstacles.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: While levitating, reduce incoming damage by 30%.

Unite Move.Name: Compute and Crush
Unite Move.Description: Quickly analyzes the position of nearby enemies, granting a shield based on the number of enemies analyzed and become unstoppable. When 2 or fewer enemies are analyzed, lunge towards the enemy with the lowest HP, leaving it unable to act for 0.8s and dealing damage to it thrice, with the third hit dealing damage based on the target's missing HP. If an enemy is knocked out by this move, the user recovers 30% of its Unite Move gauge. If there are three or more analyzed, lunge toward the enemy with the lowest HP, dealing damage to nearby enemies and creating a wall around the user. If a wall is created, Compute and Crash may be used again to take down the wall.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 30% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Beast Boost
Passive Ability.Description: When Buzzwole knocks out an enemy player, movement speed is increased by 100% and basic attack speed increased by 50% for 3s. When Buzzwole assists in knocking out an enemy player, movement speed is increased by 50% and basic attack speed increased by 25% for 3s. Each time Buzzwole hits an enemy with basic attacks, Mega Punch, Lunge, or Smack Down - muscle gauge increases. Conversely, when Buzzwole has been out of combat for 10s, muscle gauge depletes by 1 increment every 5s. Up to 6 muscle gauge increments can be stored.

Buzzwole.Attack: Each time a basic attack hits an enemy, increase muscle gauge by 1 increment. Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, throwing two consecutive punches while restoring Buzzwole's HP and increasing muscle gauge by 1 increment for each hit.

Move 1.Name: Mega Punch
Move 1.Description: Jump in the designated direction before slamming the ground with a powerful punch. The shock from the punch deals damage to enemies in the area. Muscle gauge increases by 1 increment for each target hit.

Upgrade 1.Name: Lunge
Upgrade 1.Description: Lunge in the designated direction, dealing damage to enemies, decreasing their Attack by 30% for 2.5s and slowing them by 30% for 1s. If this move deals damage, muscle gauge increases by 1 increment for each enemy hit. After this move is used, Buzzwole's basic attack speed is increased by 30% for 2s. Holds 2 uses in reserve and cannot be used more than once every 1s.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Whenever this move hits enemy players, Buzzwole's Attack increases by 10% for 2s for each player hit (up to a max of 5 stacks).

Upgrade 2.Name: Smack Down
Upgrade 2.Description:
Deliver an uppercut punch in the designated direction. The first Pokémon hit takes damage and is thrown into the air, ending with a slam on the ground that deals damage to all enemies in an area centered on the previously airborne enemy. Muscle gauge increases by 1 increment for each enemy damaged by the initial hit and slam.

For a time after the initial hit, buzzwole can activate this move again to teleport to the target and slam them into the ground at their current position, ending the airborne travel instantly.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Widens the area-of-effect in which damage is dealt when the grappled enemy slams the ground. Additionally, muscle gauge will now increase by 2 increments for each enemy player hit from the slam.

Move 2.Name: Fell Stinger
Move 2.Description: Consumes the entire muscle gauge and has the user deal damage to an enemy in the designated direction, briefly shoving and stunning them for .2s. The damage dealt by this move increases for each muscle gauge increment consumed at a rate of 12.5% per increment. If this move knocks out any Pokémon, the user recovers 10% of maximum HP.

Upgrade 1.Name: Leech Life
Upgrade 1.Description:
Grab and restrain an enemy in the designated direction, becoming unstoppable and dealing damage-over-time while restoring Buzzwole's HP. When this move is used, consume the user's entire muscle gauge. The number of hits and the amount of damage dealt per hit increases for each muscle gauge increment consumed. Number of hits starts at 4 and increases by 1 per 2 increments of muscle gauge consumed. Initial damage increases by 1.5% per muscle gauge increment. This move ends if Buzzwole becomes unable to act for any reason or if the restrained enemy escapes by using an Eject Button or through some other means.

Against Unstoppable Targets: Only 1 instance of increased damage and healing will occur.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Also causes each instance of damage dealt by this move to be stronger than the last by 5% additively.

Upgrade 2.Name: Superpower
Upgrade 2.Description:
Consumes the entire muscle gauge and has the user run in the designated direction and grab an enemy. The user then slams the target down on the ground at full strength, dealing damage to all enemies in the area. If Buzzwole is successful in grabbing a target, Buzzwole receives a shield that lasts for 2s. Buzzwole is unstoppable during this move.

The damage dealt by this move is increased at a rate of 12.5% per muscle gauge increment.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Reduces the cooldown of this move by 3s when the user's muscle gauge reaches maximum.

Unite Move.Name: Ultra Swole Slam
Unite Move.Description:
Buzzwole flies at high speed, dealing damage to enemies in the area-of-effect. Afterward, the user targets the enemy with the lowest percentage of HP remaining among those hit and charges down at them from the sky, dealing area damage once again and additional damage to the targeted enemy based on their missing HP. Buzzwole is Unstoppable during this move.

Against Wild Pokemon: Missing HP damage is not dealt.
Unite Move.Buff Stats: 30% Movement Speed, 30% CDR, 20% Max HP Shield

Passive Ability.Name: Gooey
Passive Ability.Description:
When this Pokémon is hit by an attack, there is a chance it will spread slime around itself. The slime deals damage to Pokémon, Decreases their attack speed by 50% for 2s and applies a Gooey debuff. The Gooey debuff slows Pokémon by 10% for 6s and can stack up to 4 times per Pokémon. Gooey is a debuff that is applicable to Wild Pokémon and enemy players only.

When this Pokémon enters tall grass, it continually recovers HP for up to 3s, until it leaves the tall grass, uses a move, or attacks. When this Pokémon recovers HP due to Gooey, its attack gauge is progressed towards a boosted attack. This HP recovery goes on a 12s cooldown after being triggered.

Goodra.Attack:
Becomes a boosted attack with every third attack, dealing increased damage. Hitting a Pokémon afflicted with a Gooey effect with a basic attack reduces all of this Pokémon's move cooldowns by 1.5s.

Boosted attacks spew slime and if the slime hits Pokémon from a distance, a Gooey debuff is applied to those Pokémon and slows by 50% for 0.5s. If the boosted attack is used on a Pokémon in regular attack melee range, the attack deals increased damage.

Move 1.Name: Bubble
Move 1.Description: Cover itself in a bubble, granting it a shield for 3s. When the shield’s effect duration ends or if this move is used again, the bubble shield pops, dealing damage to nearby opposing Pokémon and slowing them by 30% for 2s.

Upgrade 1.Name: Muddy Water
Upgrade 1.Description: Splashes muddy water around itself, dealing damage to opposing Pokémon in the area of effect and applying an effect to them that decreases the amount of damage they deal by 15% for 3s. If the user deals damage to opposing Pokémon with this move, the user’s Defense and Sp. Def are increased for 2s. If Muddy Water is used within 6 seconds, the defense bonus effect is increased in intensity. The defenses bonus effect can stack up to four times.
Upgrade 1.Enhanced Description: Further increases the user’s Defense and Sp. Def when this move deals damage to opposing Pokémon.

Upgrade 2.Name: Dragon Pulse
Upgrade 2.Description:
Unleashes a shock wave in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon it hits up to 4 times. If the center of the shock wave hits enemy Pokémon, it deals increased damage to them and restores the user’s HP. More HP is restored when the center of the pulse deals damage to enemy Pokémon that have a Gooey slowing effect applied to them.

The fourth tick of damage can be animation cancelled by inputs (attack or movement). It is undetermined if this is a bug or not.
Upgrade 2.Enhanced Description: Restores more HP if the center of the shock wave hits Pokémon, and even more on Pokémon that have a Gooey debuff applied to them.

Move 2.Name: Tackle
Move 2.Description: Charges at a Pokémon, dealing damage and throwing it. If this move hits, the user bounces back a short distance.

Upgrade 1.Name: Power Whip
Upgrade 1.Description: Lashes out with its horns in the designated direction, dealing damage to Pokémon in the area of effect and slowing them by 80% for 3s, decaying by 15% every 0.5s. If this move hits distant Pokémon, those Pokémon are pulled toward Goodra.
